programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team are called in when a Navy reservist returns home to find her husband missing and her living room covered in blood, and make a grim discovery when they find a finger in the bin. Mark Harmon stars.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: New Mossad director Orli Elbaz asks for the team's help in finding Ilan Bodnar when one of her agents is shot dead in Virginia, telling Gibbs he was investigating several operatives who had gone rogue after Eli David's death. Ziva travels with Tony to Berlin in her pursuit of Bodnar, the man she believes was responsible for the death of her father. Guest starring Marina Sirtis (Star Trek: The Next Generation).(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tracks Bodnar's SUV to a remote house in the countryside, but inside find only his dead accomplice - identified as a South African mercenary who helped Bodnar procure diamonds. Ziva and Tony, meanwhile, discharge themselves from hospital and wheedle information out of Abby to continue investigating on the quiet, having been warned by Homeland Security to stay off the case.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A cook from a US Navy destroyer tells McGee and Gibbs he believes someone is stalking him with the intention of trying to make him poison the crew, and a picture on a stolen phone leads the team to a group testing the psychological effects of surveillance. Meanwhile, Richard Parsons from the Inspector General's office closes in on Ziva as he investigates the death of Ilan Bodnar, the man who killed her father. Guest starring Colin Hanks (Gus Grimly in the Fargo TV series).(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates when Navy Seal Chad McBride's wife receives a package from Riyadh in Saudi Arabia containing her husband's severed head, which Palmer discovers contains very high levels of radiation. McBride's commanding officer reveals the soldier was on a top-secret reconnaissance mission and asks Vance not to disclose the details to anyone. Meanwhile, Richard Parsons tries to get Gibbs to take responsibility for the many unnecessary deaths for which he holds the NCIS team accountable.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Lance Powell goes undercover in a gay bar in a successful operation to catch a dealer selling lethal crystal meth, but his personal life takes a knock when partner Mark announces he's leaving the Met. Difficult decisions have to be made about a young tearaway when he's accused of mugging an old man, and a remorseful Terry reconsiders his relationship with Lucy after letting Bradley down yet again.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: A Small Piece of the Pie
programme.desc: Neil Manson returns to Sun Hill to work on the murder of a gay man and Phil Hunter spots a pattern in similar recent crimes. Lance Powell teams up with Laura Bryant to investigate a spate of robberies for which the local vicar turns out to be the chief suspect. Mark leaves his partner distraught when he moves out to find some space, and Cindy urges her reluctant husband to open the results of the paternity test.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: When Justice Isn't Enough - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of three. An anonymous caller alerts Phil Hunter to common threads in recent murders, and the team starts looking for a serial killer on the Sun Hill gay scene. Lance Powell struggles to come to terms with Mark's decision to take time out from their relationship and decides to drown his sorrows, but a visit to the gay bar he recently investigated puts him in grave danger.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: When Justice Isn't Enough - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of three. The shocked Sun Hill relief pull out all the stops to find the killer of popular colleague Lance Powell. As a tearful Laura Bryant is sent home on compassionate leave, a witness provides a breakthrough, and the trail of evidence leads back to Michael Keenan, the barman at gay club Radar. Amid the emotional turmoil, Phil Hunter accompanies Kate as she registers the birth of their son, and is devastated to learn she's leaving to live with her mother in Cardiff.(n)

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Period medical drama chronicling the personal and professional consequences of Eleanor Bramwell's determined quest to become the first female surgeon at the East London Hospital. Jemma Redgrave stars.(n)

programme.desc: Drama about one man's journey through love and war in the early part of the century. Charlie MacFell returns from university to the family farm, where his strict father still rules with an iron fist _ a situation that soon changes thanks to a misunderstanding with a foolish farmhand. Lloyd Owen stars, with Catherine Zeta Jones, Tom Bell and Maria Mile.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Ash Park prepares for a visit from the Prime Minister and his wife in support of George's election campaign, and James begins to learn how to relax in public with Harry.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: On the day of the Prime Minister's visit to Ash Park, Regina is in her element making preparations and George is happy to make a speech to his supporters gathered at the gates. Sarah, knowing that she would not be welcome, awaits Harry at Inverness hospital from his transfer from Sydney. Elizabeth, meanwhile, has to time her return home with military precision.(n)

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth is waiting for a new three-piece suite to be delivered and goes to extraordinary lengths to ensure her neighbours see the royal warrant on the delivery van. But, as ever, her family contrives to take the gloss off her moment of glory. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge.(n)

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth decides to take Daddy out for a picnic, but her plans soon backfire. Suburban comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: While Richard contends with the idea of taking early retirement, Hyacinth telephones the prime minister asking if there is anything on the honours list for him. The hen-pecked husband's reservations about the future intensify when he discovers all the plans his spouse has in store for him. Comedy, starring Clive Swift and Patricia Routledge.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Kris Marshall is back as the British detective working on the Caribbean island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. And so it appears with the first case when a plantation owner ends up dead during a seance - although with the door firmly closed at the time and all the participants holding hands round the table, who did the deed? The victim's family are convinced it was the work of a ghost said to haunt the plantation, but Humphrey is determined to find a more rational explanation. Meanwhile, Fidel has moved on, leaving Officer Dwayne Myers holding the fort - and while he is struggling to cope, the last thing he needs is the Commissioner stepping in to help. Crime drama, with guest appearances from Holby City's Don Gilet and former Corrie bad girl Natalie Gumede.(n)

programme.title: Harrow
programme.desc: While Grace autopsies the body of an elderly man discovered in an Egyptian sarcophagus, Harrow finds himself trapped in a battle for his own life at the hands of Chester. Australian drama, starring Ioan Gruffudd and Jolene Anderson.(n)

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: The leader of a ship workers' union is shot dead in the middle of a labour dispute and his boss sustains gunshot wounds. Burke and Reid initially believe they may have a corporate killing on their hands, but the case remains far from clear until alarming evidence is uncovered that brings everything into sharp focus. Detective drama, guest starring Gordon Kennedy and Joanna Roth.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad reopens the investigation into an actress's disappearance when clips of her being assaulted in a 1990 movie are posted on the internet. Director Don Maddox features in the footage, but when questioned, he appears to have problems distinguishing fact from fiction. A potential witness is then found dead, and Brian and Jack are seconded to the unit handling the case, only to find Emily Driscoll (Dennis Waterman's daughter Hannah) is heading the operation. Imogen Stubbs and Sandra Dickinson also guest star.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad reinvestigates the death of a timeshare magnate, previously thought to have committed suicide, when a reformed call girl claims she was with him the night he died and saw someone force their way into his house as she left. It then emerges the deceased's widow has since fallen on hard times after being swindled by her late husband's business partner - who is connected to a notorious criminal. Guest starring Shane Richie, Naomi Bentley and Claire Rushbrook.(n)

Meanwhile, Charlie keeps a watchful eye on the cocky new receptionist, and Josh finally plucks up the courage to ask Colette for a date. Sandra Huggett and Ian Kelsey star.(n)

programme.desc: The Sun Hill relief bid a final farewell to murdered colleague Lance Powell and Adam Okaro's funeral address is all the more moving for a spell on the beat alongside Laura Bryant. A routine drugs bust turns into a free-for-all when the dealer's henchmen turn up in numbers at the critical moment.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Furious about the recent run-in with Jimmy, Grant is ripe for revenge. Ricky discovers that his work brings him closer to home. Mark gives Michelle some brotherly advice.(n)

programme.desc: Sharon joins a video dating agency and asks Darren to shoot a rehearsal tape, while Garth buys tickets for a Rolling Stones concert.(n)

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Audrey injures her back, but soon recovers when DeVere invites her on a skiing trip. However, when the entrepreneur gives her advance lessons on the estate, he manages to end up the invalid. Comedy, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: The Last Surviving Maurice Chevalier Impression
programme.desc: Compo decides the way to win the heart of Nora Batty is to become a celebrity, so goes on a televised talent show. Gorden Kaye ('Allo 'Allo!) guest stars, alongside Bill Owen, Peter Sallis and Brian Wilde.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy tries to defy his age by entering the annual barrel-rolling competition, and undergoes a gruelling training programme. Meanwhile, Pearl buys Howard a tropical fish tank as a gift. Long-running rural comedy, starring Brian Wilde and Juliette Kaplan.(n)

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detectives are called out to a robbery where a security guard is found dead. As they try to determine whether his demise was an accident or murder, they expose a complex web of deception among his friends - or enemies. Meanwhile, Lateef uncovers evidence of an international exchange racket. Art Malik guest stars.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: UCOS looks into the murder of a private investigator who was stabbed through the heart in a suspected robbery gone wrong. The answers may lie with the victim's last three cases, but it'll take the whole team's expertise to crack the codes within his mysterious notebook. The movements of a rare stamp could hold the key to the investigation - if only UCOS could track it down. Meanwhile, Steve has problems when he rents out his flat.(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team takes on three cases at once in-between major murder investigations. Harry deals with a hit-and-run on a council estate, Leo investigates the cause of a fire that left a woman dead, and Nikki is called out when a bride-to-be collapses and dies on her hen night. Starring Tom Ward, William Gaminara and Emilia Fox.(n)

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: Sequel to The White Queen, set in the Middle Ages and picking up as the tumultuous War of the Roses comes to an end, a new generation ascends to the throne in a tale of power, political clashes, family, love and betrayal. As the victor of the Battle of Bosworth, Henry Tudor, is set to ascend the throne and Elizabeth of York and her family, supporters of the former King, find themselves in grave danger. Lizzie must fulfil her promise to marry King Henry VII and bring peace to the nation. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star.(n)

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: King Henry VII sets out to establish himself as the new sovereign, but finds a dangerous, deeply divided kingdom. Lizzie's loyalties are further complicated by the child she is expecting. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star.(n)

programme.desc: The Sun Hill relief bid a final farewell to murdered colleague Lance Powell and Adam Okaro's funeral address is all the more moving for a spell on the beat alongside Laura Bryant. Nadir and Sim investigate a well known footballer accused of rape and discover he has form - as does the witness who gave his name to the police. A routine drugs bust turns into a free-for-all when the dealer's henchmen turn up in numbers at the critical moment.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Donna hands in her notice, but insists that the lottery win has not changed her. Sam tells Jade everything will work out, oblivious to the gossip going around the hospital. Meanwhile, Matt ignores Dean's advice and goes out for a drink with Nikki after the pair meet when he treats her sick father.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Patrick has little time for celebration when Duffy takes her maternity leave, especially once a retired doctor arrives at the hospital and starts putting him through his paces. Medical drama, guest starring Frank Thornton, with Ian Kelsey and Cathy Shipton.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: New boy PC Will Fletcher (Gary Lucy) gets off to a bad start at Sun Hill as he's stopped for speeding on the way, reprimanded for being late and put in his place for an inappropriate remark about a member of the public. He starts to feel more at home after a successful operation and a wind-up by Smithy and Tony Stamp. Things start to turn sour for Terry Perkins when he visits his brother in prison.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: After another exam Michelle finds unexpected help in getting to Pete's inquest, whilst Kathy leaves Natalie and Bianca in charge of the cafe - with chaotic results.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil decides it's time to sort Jimmy out. Alan wants to take more responsibility for his grandmother, but Carol isn't too happy about it.(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: A huge blaze in a bus depot gives Blue Watch one of their toughest challenges. Carole gets some good news about her future.(n)

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Alice appears in court for non-payment of council tax, a teenager asks Jack for tranquilisers, and interviews are held for the post of fund manager. Medical drama, starring Kevin Whately.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Jack Clayton (ex-Emmerdale actor Ian Kelsey) and his family visit Skelthwaite, the home town of his wife Nisha (former Coronation Street actress Shobna Gulati), who happens to be a nurse - handy for Anna, who is struggling to replace Kim. David doesn't feel like celebrating his birthday as the factory's problems reach crisis point, and Zoe becomes suspicious of a patient's boyfriend.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The girls' aunt is taken to hospital with a broken leg, and it's up to them to decide whether she goes to an old people's home or comes to stay at their house. Comedy, starring Linda Robson and Pauline Quirke.(n)

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Audrey relishes her role as country commissioner for the Girl Guides when she reprimands Richard for not sponsoring a fundraising event in aid of a new scout hut. Comedy, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The wily trio set about trying to find a new home for Smiler, whose wife has left him after 33 years of marriage and gone to live with her sister in Australia.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo is very upset to learn that that Nora Batty has taken Smiler as a lodger, so Foggy attempts to distract him from his depression by indulging in a spot of dancing. Brian Wilde and Bill Owen star.(n)

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: A black man is murdered in a jail cell by a confirmed racist - a crime the prison service blames on overcrowding. However, the judge's probing reveals the institution wasn't operating at full capacity. A drink-driving appeal puts the bench at loggerheads, and Jo distances herself from her former lover. With Donald Sinden and Jenny Seagrove.(n)

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Charlie is appointed to defend an animal rights activist accused of murdering an eminent scientist - a case that will be heard before her father. The defence barrister's support for her client's cause leads her to make a series of unprofessional outbursts in court, while Jo's relationship with Marc Thompson creates tension.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A skeleton uncovered in the foundations of a swimming pool is identified as a young lottery winner named Cheryl, who was reported missing 17 years ago. The prime suspects are her fellow quiz team-mates, who won their lucky windfall as part of a syndicate, and while some have put their money to good use, the victim's boyfriend has fallen on hard times and the media remain convinced he is the key to her murder. As allegations of affairs, fights and betrayal begin to surface, the team is left to ponder whether money really is the root of all evil.(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Harry's burgeoning relationship with Nikki is jeopardised when his former girlfriend Penny is killed in a car crash. programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: The undercover cameras reveal how animals rely upon each other for a range of activities. Arctic wolves group together to survive in one of the harshest landscapes on earth, a band of hungry mongoose groom warthogs for tasty morsels - before turning their attention to Spy Warthog - and fish help clean hippos' bodies, as well as providing a unique dental service. Meanwhile, crocodiles and dikkop birds take part in a mutual neighbourhood watch against hungry prowling monitor lizards, and Spy Rattlesnake captures how prairie dogs and burrowing owls use their language skills to tell the rest of their community of approaching danger.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani visits Epping Forest to find out about its past, its wildlife and to meet some of the people who look after it. She also discovers the art of 'cattle whispering' when she meets the herd of longhorns that help to conserve the Forest and, at dusk, she's on the trail of one of the UK's rarest creatures. Wildlife film-maker Hamza Yassin gets up close to a much-loved seabird, the puffin, near his home on the west coast of Scotland, while Gareth Wyn Jones offers a glimpse into his life as a hill farmer in Wales. Down on his own farm, Adam Henson has a shearing dilemma to solve.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Cromer seafront in Norfolk. Items brought in to be valued include two pieces of Italian jewellery, a Chinese vase, and a silver goblet that survived the Great Fire of London in perfect condition. Philip Mould examines a pencil sketch by LS Lowry to determine if it is real or a fake.(n)

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: Period mystery set in 19th-century New Zealand at the height of the 1860s gold rush, and based on the Man Booker Prize-winning novel by Eleanor Catton. Politician Alistair Lauderback arrives at a cottage to find a woman, unconscious with opium, a Maori man - also unconscious - and a corpse. Lauderback brings the woman to town, where she is arrested for public intoxication and held at the jail. Nine months earlier, Anna Wetherell arrives in New Zealand. After a romantic first encounter, she agrees to meet with fellow passenger Emery Staines, but scheming fortune-teller Lydia Wells has other ideas. Starring Eva Green, Eve Hewson, Benedict Hardie and Himesh Patel.(n)

programme.desc: Daniel De Simone investigates a global network of neo-Nazis that aims to destroy society, discovering that it is recruiting in the UK. Police say right-wing extremism is the fastest growing terrorist threat in the UK and that the coronavirus pandemic may be leaving young people vulnerable to radicalisation. As Daniel investigates he notices certain names cropping up again and again and tracks down some of the movement's most influential figures and reveals how the network operates across the globe.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by some of Walford's biggest stars to relive their characters' most iconic moments, share behind-the-scenes stories and reveal stuff that is normally kept under wraps. She begins by catching up with Danny Dyer and Kellie Bright - Mick and Linda Carter - who talk about an embarrassing wardrobe malfunction and give Stacey a tour of the Queen Vic. Danny also shares his best 'doof doof' face, and reveals which EastEnders legend sent him a special note before he took over the pub.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: Anna Wetherell is now working at Lydia Wells' fortune parlour, The House of Many Wishes, where she learns that Lydia is plotting with Francis Carver to steal from Crosbie. Carver deceives Emery Staines into thinking that Anna has left for the goldfields. Emery goes to follow her, first agreeing to split his future goldfield winnings with Carver, registering their joint claim under the name 'Aurora'. Emery almost drowns upon his arrival on the Hokitika goldfields, and believing Anna to have drowned as well, he befriends a Maori man named Te Rau Tauwhare, and heads into the hills to grieve.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A classic episode of the soap from 1986 directed by acclaimed film-maker Antonia Bird, which was the first episode of the show to feature only two characters. Den Watts has demanded a divorce from his wife Angie, who resorts to desperate measures to ensure he does not leave her. Starring Anita Dobson and Leslie Grantham.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Fletch's surgery is derailed when Evie reveals a secret that makes him question himself, but both suspect they are just making excuses out of a fear of what the future may hold. Nicky treats a familiar face, and is put in a difficult position by her mother that forces her to choose between cutting her off or making a promise she cannot keep. Max's big day is ruined by devastating revelations from three of her colleagues.(n)

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Talking Heads
programme.desc: New performances of Alan Bennett's monologues. Imelda Staunton performs A Lady of Letters, the story of a woman who compulsively spies on her neighbours. She becomes fascinated by a little girl who lives across the road, who displays some unusual bruises. But when she begins sending aggressive letters to the child's parents, it leads her into trouble with the police.(n)

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Talking Heads
programme.desc: Sarah Lancashire performs Alan Bennett's monologue An Ordinary Woman, the story of a mother who finds herself developing incestuous desires for her own 15-year-old son. She feels unable to confide in her husband and sees herself has too normal to admit to anything so shocking, but things are brought to a head by the revelation that her son has a girlfriend.(n)

programme.title: I May Destroy You
programme.desc: Arabella attends a support group for rape and sexual assault survivors run by old high school acquaintance Theo. Terry has misgivings about the group owing to an incident in their teenage years. Flashbacks to 2004 reveal a moment where Arabella and Terry had to intervene when Theo tried to flip the narrative on consensual sex gone sideways.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: It's a busy weekend for the medics as thousands of visitors flood into the city for the Grand National weekend - and first up is a visitor falling down some steps at the train station. Mo and Tony are called to a baby that has fallen from its pram, and soon realise the mother is under the influence of alcohol, while Anthony and Mandy are dispatched to a nursing home where a patient is confused state fainting. Paramedics and sisters Danielle and Stacey treat the first casualty at the racecourse - a jockey who has broken his ankle falling from his horse, meaning he will have to miss the big race the following day.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode's alphabetical tour of the BBC's culinary archive reaches the letter O. Jamie Oliver prepares baked onions and Michel Roux rustles up a pear omelette souffle with salted butter and caramel sauce.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: As well as tending to his crops, much of Adam Henson's time on his Cotswold farm is devoted to caring for his animals. Each and every one of them is important to him, from his commercial flock of sheep to his chickens, and from his pigs to his ponies, with many of them having become much-loved characters to the show's viewers. This special edition of Countryfile takes a journey into the archives to revisit and rediscover some of Adam's highs and lows with his marvellous menagerie of creatures.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don gives tips on growing tomatoes and has ideas of how to fill gaps in the garden once the flowers of spring are over. Joe Swift visits the small city garden of an interior designer where some unusual plants have been used to add an element of surprise to the outdoor space, while in Kent Arit Anderson meets a community whose members have all been prescribed gardening as part of their recovery from physical and mental illness.(n)

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: The team celebrates the Royal Highland Show, which has been cancelled this year. Dougie Vipond charts its 180-year history and Arlene Stuart hears from show regulars at home.(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: George demonstrates how to detect and deal with garden pests and Kirsty Wilson creates an indoor succulent tower.(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which leading food experts take Fred Sirieix to their favourite restaurants to explore what makes them so special. Here, Fred heads to Marrakesh in the company of TV chef Andi Oliver, whose love of soul food has led to an obsession with the city's rich culinary heritage. The maitre d' reveals he has been to Marrakesh before but has yet to have a truly memorable meal. Can Andi change his mind as they are served lamb and couscous at a legendary family restaurant, chicken tagine for under £10, and breakfast on a spectacular roof terrace?(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: Actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from TV chefs, including the likes of Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson and Lorraine Pascale.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Rory Bremner presents a profile of Barry Norman, the respected film critic, journalist and TV presenter who fronted the BBC's Film programme from 1972 to 1998, and died in June.(n)

programme.title: The Magnificent Seven (1960)
programme.desc: Oppressed Mexican villagers cross the border into America looking to buy guns to defend their homes and crops from bandits. Instead they encounter an enigmatic loner who persuades them to hire a band of gunslingers to teach the villagers how to fight before the marauders return. Western, with Yul Brynner, Steve McQueen, Charles Bronson, Robert Vaughn, James Coburn and Eli Wallach.(n)

programme.title: Andy Murray: King of Queen's
programme.desc: A look at the tennis star's success in the Wimbledon warm-up event at Queen's Club in London, featuring footage of some of memorable matches from his five singles title wins. Murray has won a record five singles titles on the immaculate grass courts at Queen's. It was the scene of his first win on the ATP Tour back in 2005, and both of his Wimbledon titles were prefaced with victory at the west London venue, which is one of the most prestigious events in men's tennis and one of the highlights of the British sporting summer. The programme features some of his biggest triumphs, including his emotional victory with Feliciano Lopez in the men's doubles there last year, just a few months after major hip surgery.(n)

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: The programme goes behind the scenes as Casino de Monte Carlo holds an exclusive event with a Prohibition theme aimed at high rollers as it seeks to attract new clients, while at the palace Prince Albert II hosts the new French ambassador. In Monaco's only tattoo parlour, Princess Stephanie's daughter Camille Gottlieb is getting a discreet new inking and sharing what life is like for her in the principality, and four billion euros' worth of motor vessels enter the harbour for the annual yacht show.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.sub-title: Inside the Factory: Keeping Britain Going
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace catches up with workers at the Typhoo Tea factory on the Wirral, which he visited back in 2017, where production has been upped to produce 109 million bags a week. In reports from the programme's archives, Cherry Healey visits a tea processing plant in Kenya, and Ruth Goodman sheds light on how Second World War troops risked their lives to brew up.(n)

programme.title: Thatcher: A Very British Revolution
programme.desc: The rise and dramatic downfall of Margaret Thatcher, as told by members of her inner circle, who reveal how a political outsider won power and came to dominate the 1980s. The first episode charts how she managed to become a prominent political figure and survived the controversial policy that saw the press label her the 'milk snatcher', before challenging Edward Heath for the leadership of the Conservative Party.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den: Best Ever Pitches
programme.desc: Joe Lycett narrates a collection of memorable moments in which contestants made unusual efforts to win over the panel, from serenading them to staging a zombie attack. The brains behind a musical gift service favours red roses and flattery, while the inventor of a voice coaching massage chair hopes his creation will relax them into submission. However, the show's most successful entrant, Levi Roots, knows the best way to the Dragons' wallets is through their stomachs.(n)

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Investigates
programme.desc: A report on South Korea's trade in illegal spy camera footage. Pornography has been made illegal in the country, which has led criminals to begin a lucrative trade filming people without their knowledge and distributing the films to strangers or streaming it live on the internet. Stacey investigates the rising number of suicides among victims, examines how the authorities are trying to crack down on this rapidly evolving crime, and questions if the practice could take root in the UK.(n)

programme.title: When Louis Met...
programme.desc: Documentary in which Louis Theroux follows Neil and Christine Hamilton through their wrongful arrest on serious sexual assault charges - allowing him unprecedented access as the scandal and drama unfolds.(n)

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: Bishop's men face a difficult choice between seeking revenge and making a profit. Felipe reveals to his sons some past cartel connections.(n)

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: EZ and Angel both have scores to settle and sins to be punished for - but forgiving an enemy may be the hardest challenge they face.(n)

programme.title: Leviathan (2014)
programme.desc: A mechanic comes under pressure from the Mayor of his small hometown, who wants to own the land his repair shop is built on. In desperation, the mechanic turns to a lawyer for help, unaware that this decision will have devastating repercussions for his life. Drama, starring Aleksey Serebryakov and Elena Lyadova. In Russian.(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from viewers at home on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian struggles to cope as a patient dredges up the past, threatening his relationship with Jac, who has uncovered something suspicious concerning Kestrel. Louis works with Guy on a case, only to fall out when he discovers he has been the subject of hospital gossip. Fletch awaits a confirmed date for his operation, only to cause chaos when he makes a false assumption concerning a garish gift.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The red and blue teams hunt for items in Dorking, with the help of experts Caroline Hawley and Ochuko Ojiri. Presenter Eric Knowles also learns about a famous local resident.(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Adventures
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull continues their travels around Britain, this time seeking out the birthplaces of the inventions that fuelled the nation's industrial infrastructure. Their first stop is the Royal Institution in London, where they examine how the Davy lamp saved the lives of thousands of coal miners, then pay a visit to the preserved laboratory of Michael Faraday, another of their scientific heroes, whose research on electricity paved the way for its application as a ubiquitous energy supply.(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock.(n)

programme.title: The Victorian Slum
programme.desc: Michael Mosley follows the progress of a group of people experiencing the tough living and working conditions of the Victorian poor after moving into a restored Victorian tenement in the heart of London's East End. In the opening edition the slum dwellers move into their new home, a building made up of sparse rooms, a shared water pump and outdoor privy toilets, while one finds out first-hand the back breaking labour his forebears would have experienced and the effect on a Victorian family when the main breadwinner was out of action.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Chase the Case
programme.desc: Strategic game show hosted by Dan Walker, in which five contestants trade general knowledge for information to gain access to a case containing a secret amount of cash.(n)

programme.title: Euros Rewind
programme.desc: Netherlands v Denmark. A chance to see the Euro 92 semi-final at Ullevi in Gothenburg, Sweden.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Milestones Museum in Basingstoke, alongside experts James Lewis and Elizabeth Talbot. Featuring a Hampshire sculptor who carves giant spiders.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson presents a look back at some of the finest country homes featured in Escape to the Country over the past five years. He begins with a selection of beautiful Georgian and Victorian property tours, two magnificent eras of architecture that have stood the test of time. This collection includes a range of budgets from modest to million-pound-plus, but all homes are united by their by sumptuous, period elegance.(n)

programme.title: Thatcher: A Very British Revolution
programme.desc: Margaret Thatcher's victory in the 1979 general election propels her into power as Britain's first female prime minister, but her first two years are characterised by disruption and division. Her first cabinet is split between a small group aligned with her plans for radical change, but the majority are experienced ministers who prefer a more consensual style of politics. She also has to deal with an economy in deep trouble, with inflation rising, unemployment spiralling and public spending growing.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.sub-title: Inside the Factory: Keeping Britain Going
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace checks in with one of Britain's biggest biscuit factories, chatting to some of the workers via video calls to find out how they are coping during the Coronavirus pandemic. Gregg speaks to manager Nina Sparks, who he met during his visit back in 2017. Nina reassures him that all the ingredients for the biscuits are still getting through, including the flour that has been missing from many supermarket shelves due to a packaging shortage. Nina agrees to be Gregg's 'roving reporter', to show him what's changed on the production lines to meet demand, and what new safety measures have been put in place to keep the staff safe.(n)

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: The final episode of the documentary features a tour of an extraordinary new home - Villa Troglodyte, which has come onto the market. Built as if carved out of the rock, the price is 'only' £23million because it doesn't have a sea view. The pressure to build more houses in such a small country has left Prince Albert with a challenge. Cameras follow him as he attends meetings about a huge two-billion-euro land extension project he has initiated. Extending into the sea is a complex operation, but once complete the largest villas there are estimated to sell for around 100million euros.(n)

programme.title: Comedians: Home Alone
programme.desc: More comedians perform a selection of quickfire sketches, songs and isolation anecdotes, filmed by themselves in and around their own homes during the coronavirus locdown. Tom Allen offers some quarantine etiquette tips, Jason Manford demonstrates how to cook healthily for six kids, and Meera Syal and Sanjeev Bhaskar revisit The Kumars. Plus, Bob Mortimer's viral hit Train Guy is animated for the first time, and Tim Key performs more seclusion poetry.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Part one of two. Celebrating LGBT+ stand-up comedy at the BBC, showcasing the funniest moments from stars who have appeared on Live at the Apollo. This edition features sets from Fern Brady, Desiree Burch, Alan Carr, Larry Dean, Eddie Izzard, Joe Lycett and Zoe Lyons.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Stephen: The Murder That Changed a Nation
programme.desc: Documentary examining the 1993 death of Stephen Lawrence, beginning with a look at events leading up to his murder and the police investigation that follows. As the suspects remain free, tip-offs from the community make his parents, Doreen and Neville, wonder why the authorities are not making arrests.(n)

programme.title: A House Through Time
programme.desc: David Olusoga traces the lives of 10 Guinea Street's occupants through the Second World War to the present day, discovering stories of love, loss and renewal. In the run-up to the war, the house is occupied by the younger generation of the Wallingtons, the long-term owners of number 10, who move back to the house after several years living in the rural Somerset. The Wallingtons are letting rooms to lodgers when war is declared in 1939, and would have sheltered in their cellar as German bombs rained down on Bristol. Over the 10 months of the Bristol Blitz, the ancient city is devastated and 3,000 houses destroyed. Number 10 itself is almost among them, as a bomb falls directly on Guinea Street, only missing the house by a whisker.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: The return of the award-winning documentary series following the work of the North West Ambulance Service as they care for the people of Liverpool and all across Merseyside. The shift begins with a call-out to a stabbing in a park - the 36th such incident the Merseyside teams have deal with in the past seven days. Then the medics prepare for a busy weekend as the St Patrick's Day celebrations hit the streets of Liverpool. Before long crewmates Paul and Emma are dispatched to a patient with chest pain, before treating another with a suspected broken arm. As the party gets into full swing, a call comes in from two children whose father has lost consciousness, and they have to be talked through the steps to revive him.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: As well as tending to his crops, much of Adam Henson's time on his Cotswold farm is devoted to caring for his animals. Each and every one of them is important to him, from his commercial flock of sheep to his chickens, and from his pigs to his ponies, with many of them having become much-loved characters to the show's viewers. This special edition of Countryfile takes a journey into the archives to revisit and rediscover some of Adam's highs and lows with his marvellous menagerie of creatures.(n)

programme.title: Saving Our Nurses
programme.desc: Documentary following a mentorship scheme designed to help new nurses stay in the profession and reverse a trend in which more than a third are looking for new roles. Newly qualified nurse Lauren has reached breaking point in one of the busiest A&E departments in the UK, so mentor Taz tries to negotiate an urgent move for her to a new part of the hospital.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Gillian Burke, Iolo Williams and Gordon Buchanan share more seasonal stories from Spring 2020. Gillian reveals the extraordinary camouflage tactics of some rock pool inhabitants, Chris reveals how snails can use their shells to help them remember, and naturalist Brett Westwood celebrates the diversity of insects in his back garden including an egg shooting fly.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Sarah Beynon hunts for dung beetles on the sea cliffs that surround Ramsey Island and finds 20th-century rubbish falling into the ocean as a dump at Lyme Regis slides into the sea.(n)

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against each other to buy four new items - a vintage bucket with a dark past, a beautiful jewellery box, two chairs that bamboozle them and an antique foot warmer.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea uses his expertise to make a dilapidated and unsafe go-kart roadworthy once again. Kirsten Ramsay faces an unusual challenge in the shape of a papier mache cat, while saddlemaker Suzie Fletcher is called upon to restore a hand-crafted leather gun slip, commissioned 30 years ago by a man as a gift for his father's 60th birthday.(n)

programme.title: The Victorian Slum
programme.desc: The volunteers experience the living and working conditions of the 1870s, which was blighted by a dire economic depression. The Potters can no longer rely on breadwinner Graham as he struggles to find work so they join forces with single parent Shazeda to try to survive by making artificial flowers, while there are new arrivals from Ireland in the form of siblings John and Maria Barker, who are horrified by the conditions that would have greeted Irish migrants to Victorian London.(n)

programme.title: Euros Rewind
programme.desc: Germany v Italy. A chance to see the Euro 2012 semi-final at National Stadium in Warsaw, Poland.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: David Harper and Paul Laidlaw hunt for items of interest in the picture galleries of the Bowes Museum at Barnard Castle in Co Durham, with finds including an illustrated book by Arthur Rackham and Edmund Dulac, and a carved dog's head.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman presents a compilation of memorable episodes in Scotland, examining the country's diverse architectural styles and featuring a couple who relocated 800 miles.(n)

programme.title: Thatcher: A Very British Revolution
programme.desc: A look at how Margaret Thatcher's struggling public image was transformed in 1982 when Argentine forces occupied the British overseas territory of the Falkland Islands in the South Atlantic, with the triumph of the British forces making her reputation in the UK and around the world. On the home front she faced another challenge to her leadership from National Union of Miners, which called a UK-wide strike, while in 1984 the IRA bombed the Grand Hotel in Brighten in an attempt to kill her and her most senior colleagues.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, architects Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison compete to win a commission to transform a garden room in a three-bedroom Victorian terraced house in Shrewsbury for Mike and Lisa. Robert suggests a structure that gives each of them their own separate spaces, while Laura attempts to blend the needs of the whole family with in a highly adaptable garden room.(n)

programme.title: The Choir: Singing for Britain
programme.desc: Gareth Malone sets out to bring the nation together through music during the Coronavirus lockdown, by getting people from all walks of life to perform a specially composed song. The first episode focuses on frontline workers, all of whom have a courageous story to tell.(n)

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Extended edition of the show, in which an eclectic mix of five celebrities go out for dinner together. When they arrive at the restaurant, they must place their bank card in the centre of the table. As the food is served and the drinks flow, they play a series of fun and revealing games, and if they win a game, they can retrieve their card. The person whose card remains at the end of the evening must pick up the bill for the whole group. Taking part in the first edition are Rylan Clark-Neal, Ed Gamble, Carol Vorderman, Ellie Taylor and Anton Du Beke.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits the city of Thessaloniki, revered as the gourmet capital of Greece and birthplace to possibly the greatest warrior general that ever lived - Alexander.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Stephen: The Murder That Changed a Nation
programme.desc: The second police investigation reveals shocking surveillance footage of the gang alleged to have killed Stephen, and with the help of their lawyer Imran Khan, Doreen and Neville Lawrence launch the first private murder prosecution in 150 years. However, when it collapses, questions about the failure of the first police investigation mount.(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: In a first for the series, two related celebrities join forces to investigate their family tree as comedian and actor Jack Whitehall and his father and occasional double-act partner and talk show co-host Michael team up to look into their past. They learn about a tragedy that left Michael's grandfather an orphan, and also follow their lineage back to Wales in the 1830s, where they find out about their Tory ancestor and his role fighting against the Chartist political and social reform movement.(n)

programme.title: Highlands: Scotland's Wild Heart
programme.desc: Following the efforts being made by scientists, conservationists and local people in the Highlands as they work to repair the damage of the past and rebuild wild landscapes and habitats. The programme meets Dave Anderson from Forestry Commission Scotland and his colleague Simon Smith as they tag and monitor red kite and osprey chicks, gaining a unique perspective of the lives of the birds before they fly the nest. Cameras also capture the efforts of the Scottish Wildcat Action Plan team, as well as Paul Lister's project to reintroduce wolves and bears to his Alladale Wilderness Reserve.(n)

programme.title: Hamlet: Royal Shakespeare Company
programme.desc: Sent by the ghost of his father to avenge his brutal death, Hamlet's mission to expose the truth is a perilous journey of madness, murder and lost love. What will ultimately become of a young man sent to kill? Paapa Essiedu plays Hamlet in Simon Godwin's critically acclaimed 2016 Royal Shakespeare Company production.(n)

programme.title: Windrush: Movement of the People
programme.desc: Contemporary dance piece created by Sharon Watson, artistic director of Phoenix Dance Theatre, which explores the arrival of Caribbean migrants to the UK. Featuring a soundtrack of calypso, jazz, gospel and reggae, as well as original music composed by Christella Litras.(n)

programme.title: A Very British Renaissance
programme.desc: Dr James Fox explores how the tension between two cultures - one courtly, classical and European and the other homegrown, innovative and vital - contributed to England's descent into civil war in the mid-17th century. Concluding episode of the programme celebrating an age that saw Britain embrace a world of cutting-edge art, literature, architecture and science.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: The Butcher family gather in Albert Square to mourn their father, but Ricky is unable to contact Bianca. Peggy and Pat are at loggerheads as they wallow in memories of Frank.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Bianca is on the streets with her four kids, while back on Albert Square Ricky is furious she didn't make it to the funeral. Pat is suspicious of Janine's motives.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Despite pleas from her children, Bianca refuses to come clean to Ricky and is forced to take desperate action. The family gather to hear Frank's will, and Pat makes a big decision.(n)

programme.title: Civilisations Stories
programme.desc: Poet Miles Chambers explores south-west England's connections to the slave trade, with Bristol responsible for the business end of it, while Bath boomed on the back of the profits made from it. Chambers, whose ancestors were taken as slaves from West Africa to the Caribbean, visits landmark sites including Dyrham Park, home to the Blackamoor statues, the mill in Saltford where Guinea kettles were made, and the grave of a slave in Henbury.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates another masterpiece of powerful clouds, rolling hills and winding river waters using only his special painting knife.(n)

programme.title: Black and British: A Forgotten History
programme.desc: Continuing his exploration of black British history, historian David Olusoga investigates further into the stories of thousands of Africans transported into slavery by the British, looking back at key historical events that turned Britain into the biggest slave-trading nation in the world, and shaped the future of Britain for black people. David learns about conflicting English laws that were implemented against the slaves and discovers more about the people who challenged these laws and contributed to the end of slavery.(n)

programme.title: Art of Persia
programme.desc: Broadcaster and journalist Samira Ahmed reveals how Iran preserved its distinctive language and culture despite the Arab conquest of Persia.(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary about the American black radical movement that rose to prominence in the 1970s against a backdrop of civil rights protests and escalating war in Vietnam. The film pieces together the story of the organisation's rise and fall using rare archival footage and personal accounts of activists, supporters, detractors, journalists, police officers and FBI informants.(n)

programme.title: Seven Ages of Britain
programme.desc: David Dimbleby examines artefacts that illustrate and reflect the tumultuous 17th century, during which the monarchy fell amid bloody civil war, Oliver Cromwell presided over the Commonwealth, the Crown was restored, and the Glorious Revolution created the constitutional arrangement Britain knows to this day. He views art depicting the execution of Charles I, along with Puritan tracts, images of the Lord Protector and re-enactments of the conflict, before considering Wren's plan for London after the Great Fire of 1666.(n)

programme.title: The Road to Palmyra
programme.desc: Historian Dan Cruickshank and photographer Don McCullin venture into the heart of war-torn Syria on a dangerous mission to document the cultural destruction wrought by Isis.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates another masterpiece of powerful clouds, rolling hills and winding river waters using only his special painting knife.(n)

programme.title: Art of Persia
programme.desc: Broadcaster and journalist Samira Ahmed reveals how Iran preserved its distinctive language and culture despite the Arab conquest of Persia.(n)

programme.title: Books That Made Britain
programme.sub-title: East Anglia: The Scene of the Crime
programme.desc: Martha Kearney investigates why East Anglia has inspired so many great crime writers, including PD James and Nicci French.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a landscape with a glowing evening sky on top of a black canvas.(n)

programme.title: Hidden Killers
programme.sub-title: Hidden Killers of the Edwardian Home
programme.desc: In the first five years of King Edward's reign there were 140,000 British patents issued, many using electricity. However, as historian Suzannah Lipscomb discovers, there was no regulation of this new energy, and cables were not insulated or appliances earthed to today's exacting standards. As a response to electrocutions and fires, asbestos was employed, but it would be some time until its own risks to health would be fully appreciated. Suzannah also looks at other dubious products associated with the era, such as the radioactive clock and arsenic soap.(n)

programme.title: Ancient Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Michael Scott uses 3D scanning technology to reveal the historical secrets of ancient Athens and tell the story of how this remarkable city created the world's first democracy. He begins his journey on the Acropolis, where, in the late sixth century BC the people of Athens overthrew a tyrant and set up a democracy, and investigates a mysterious, asymmetrical temple called the Erechtheion that sits in the shadow of the world-famous Parthenon. To the south of the city, he clambers through a silver mine where an army of slaves once toiled inside the labyrinth of narrow tunnels.(n)

programme.title: Against the Law
programme.desc: Fact-based drama based on the 1954 Montagu Trial, in which gay journalist Peter Wildeblood and his friends Lord Montagu and Michael Pitt-Rivers were tried for homosexual offences. The ensuing scandal and public outcry over the fairness of the case forced a reluctant government to set up a committee to investigate whether homosexuality should be legalised. Starring Daniel Mays and Mark Gatiss and featuring testimony from gay men who lived through those days, a retired police officer and a former psychiatric nurse.(n)

programme.title: Prejudice and Pride: The People's History of LGBTQ Britain
programme.desc: The first of a two-part documentary in which people share the items that have defined their lives in the 50 years since homosexuality was partially decriminalised in 1967. They include a rare collection of the first openly gay magazine - featuring the rising young singer David Bowie - to letters from worried parents of newly 'out' daughters and sons. Presented by Stephen K Amos and Susan Calman.(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: When Bud falls in love with a snazzy new pair of boots, he might face his toughest adventure yet - learning to tie his shoes.(n)

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: With just 2 years' experience, Scottish teenager Iona has only five months to prepare for the biggest international competition of her life, the World Junior Surf Championships in the Azores, Portugal. It's a race against time as she trains in sub-zero conditions and battles the unpredictable seas of her home shores to find the perfect wave. It's punishing but day after day Iona dons her wetsuit and dives in. But surfing off Scotland's coast is a world away from the warm, volcanic waters of the Azores, where Iona will be up against the gnarliest (treacherous) waves she has ever faced as well as some of the most experienced and fearless surfers on the planet. Determined not to let herself or her country down, will Iona master the surf - and the pressure - to come home a champion?(n)

Charlie Dimmock and Dave and Harry Rich pay her a visit and come up with ideas to transform the featureless garden on a budget of £6,000.(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: The undercover cameras reveal how animals rely upon each other for a range of activities. Arctic wolves group together to survive in one of the harshest landscapes on earth, a band of hungry mongoose groom warthogs for tasty morsels - before turning their attention to Spy Warthog - and fish help clean hippos' bodies, as well as providing a unique dental service. Meanwhile, crocodiles and dikkop birds take part in a mutual neighbourhood watch against hungry prowling monitor lizards, and Spy Rattlesnake captures how prairie dogs and burrowing owls use their language skills to tell the rest of their community of approaching danger.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani visits Epping Forest to find out about its past, its wildlife and to meet some of the people who look after it. She also discovers the art of 'cattle whispering' when she meets the herd of longhorns that help to conserve the Forest and, at dusk, she's on the trail of one of the UK's rarest creatures. Wildlife film-maker Hamza Yassin gets up close to a much-loved seabird, the puffin, near his home on the west coast of Scotland, while Gareth Wyn Jones offers a glimpse into his life as a hill farmer in Wales. Down on his own farm, Adam Henson has a shearing dilemma to solve.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Cromer seafront in Norfolk. Items brought in to be valued include two pieces of Italian jewellery, a Chinese vase, and a silver goblet that survived the Great Fire of London in perfect condition. Philip Mould examines a pencil sketch by LS Lowry to determine if it is real or a fake.(n)

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: Period mystery set in 19th-century New Zealand at the height of the 1860s gold rush, and based on the Man Booker Prize-winning novel by Eleanor Catton. Politician Alistair Lauderback arrives at a cottage to find a woman, unconscious with opium, a Maori man - also unconscious - and a corpse. Lauderback brings the woman to town, where she is arrested for public intoxication and held at the jail. Nine months earlier, Anna Wetherell arrives in New Zealand. After a romantic first encounter, she agrees to meet with fellow passenger Emery Staines, but scheming fortune-teller Lydia Wells has other ideas. Starring Eva Green, Eve Hewson, Benedict Hardie and Himesh Patel.(n)

programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the day's three Premier League fixtures, which were Everton v Liverpool at Goodison Park, Aston Villa v Chelsea at Villa Park and Newcastle United v Sheffield United at St James' Park. Jurgen Klopp's Reds were looking to record their third consecutive victory in all competitions against their bitter rivals, and the omens looked good with the Toffees' last triumph coming way back in October 2010. Villa had a similarly poor recent record against Chelsea, having lost their previous five meetings, while the Blades hadn't beaten the Magpies since November 2006.(n)

programme.desc: Daniel De Simone investigates a global network of neo-Nazis that aims to destroy society, discovering that it is recruiting in the UK. Police say right-wing extremism is the fastest growing terrorist threat in the UK and that the coronavirus pandemic may be leaving young people vulnerable to radicalisation. As Daniel investigates he notices certain names cropping up again and again and tracks down some of the movement's most influential figures and reveals how the network operates across the globe.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by some of Walford's biggest stars to relive their characters' most iconic moments, share behind-the-scenes stories and reveal stuff that is normally kept under wraps. She begins by catching up with Danny Dyer and Kellie Bright - Mick and Linda Carter - who talk about an embarrassing wardrobe malfunction and give Stacey a tour of the Queen Vic. programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: Anna Wetherell is now working at Lydia Wells' fortune parlour, The House of Many Wishes, where she learns that Lydia is plotting with Francis Carver to steal from Crosbie. Carver deceives Emery Staines into thinking that Anna has left for the goldfields. Emery goes to follow her, first agreeing to split his future goldfield winnings with Carver, registering their joint claim under the name 'Aurora'. Emery almost drowns upon his arrival on the Hokitika goldfields, and believing Anna to have drowned as well, he befriends a Maori man named Te Rau Tauwhare, and heads into the hills to grieve.(n)

programme.title: I May Destroy You
programme.desc: Arabella remembers having sex with Biagio, but wakes up next to a sleeping Zain. Kwame goes to a police station and is interviewed by a well-meaning but out-of-his-depth officer, who can't understand why Kwame doesn't know the accused's name. Arabella goes on stage to do a reading at a literary event, but instead stuns the room by declaring that Zain is a rapist under UK law. As Zain tries to leave, Terry films him on her phone, and the clip goes viral on the internet. Drama, starring Michaela Coel.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A classic episode of the soap from 1986 directed by acclaimed film-maker Antonia Bird, which was the first episode of the show to feature only two characters. Den Watts has demanded a divorce from his wife Angie, who resorts to desperate measures to ensure he does not leave her. Starring Anita Dobson and Leslie Grantham.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Fletch's surgery is derailed when Evie reveals a secret that makes him question himself, but both suspect they are just making excuses out of a fear of what the future may hold. Nicky treats a familiar face, and is put in a difficult position by her mother that forces her to choose between cutting her off or making a promise she cannot keep. Max's big day is ruined by devastating revelations from three of her colleagues.(n)

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Talking Heads
programme.desc: New performances of Alan Bennett's monologues. Imelda Staunton performs A Lady of Letters, the story of a woman who compulsively spies on her neighbours. She becomes fascinated by a little girl who lives across the road, who displays some unusual bruises. But when she begins sending aggressive letters to the child's parents, it leads her into trouble with the police.(n)

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Talking Heads
programme.desc: Sarah Lancashire performs Alan Bennett's monologue An Ordinary Woman, the story of a mother who finds herself developing incestuous desires for her own 15-year-old son. She feels unable to confide in her husband and sees herself has too normal to admit to anything so shocking, but things are brought to a head by the revelation that her son has a girlfriend.(n)

programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: The Wedding Guru
programme.desc: Wedding planner Onkar Singh Purewal returns for a new series, catering for a very wet wedding at Caerphilly Castle. The happy couple braving the weather remain blssfully unaware that the catering team has turned up without cooking oil and set the temperature of the freezer too high. To make up for this setback, Onkar decides to throw a lavish Vaisakhi party at the family hotel to celebrate the Punjabi New Year and hopefully drum up some new customers.(n)

programme.title: I May Destroy You
programme.desc: Arabella attends a support group for rape and sexual assault survivors run by old high school acquaintance Theo. Terry has misgivings about the group owing to an incident in their teenage years. Flashbacks to 2004 reveal a moment where Arabella and Terry had to intervene when Theo tried to flip the narrative on consensual sex gone sideways.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: It's a busy weekend for the medics as thousands of visitors flood into the city for the Grand National weekend - and first up is a visitor falling down some steps at the train station. Mo and Tony are called to a baby that has fallen from its pram, and soon realise the mother is under the influence of alcohol, while Anthony and Mandy are dispatched to a nursing home where a patient is confused state fainting. Paramedics and sisters Danielle and Stacey treat the first casualty at the racecourse - a jockey who has broken his ankle falling from his horse, meaning he will have to miss the big race the following day.(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Martin Geissler and Rachel Burden.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Watford v Leicester City at Vicarage Road, Brighton & Hove Albion v Arsenal at Amex Stadium, West Ham United v Wolverhampton Wanderers at London Stadium and Bournemouth v Crystal Palace at Vitality Stadium. Plus, the key moments from Friday night's fixtures - Norwich City v Southampton and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester United.(n)

programme.desc: Wales v Belgium. Another chance to see the Euro 2016 quarter-final, which took place at Stade Pierre-Mauroy in Lille, France.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich travel to St Ives in Cambridgeshire to transform the garden of social worker Bernadette.(n)

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers compete to buy a model Spitfire, a pair of gilt cherubs, two ancient stone Celtic heads and a retro pub sign from a West Midlands brewery. Presented by Nigel Havers.(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: The undercover cameras reveal how animals rely upon each other for a range of activities. Arctic wolves group together to survive in one of the harshest landscapes on earth, a band of hungry mongoose groom warthogs for tasty morsels - before turning their attention to Spy Warthog - and fish help clean hippos' bodies, as well as providing a unique dental service. Meanwhile, crocodiles and dikkop birds take part in a mutual neighbourhood watch against hungry prowling monitor lizards, and Spy Rattlesnake captures how prairie dogs and burrowing owls use their language skills to tell the rest of their community of approaching danger.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani visits Epping Forest to find out about its past, its wildlife and to meet some of the people who look after it. She also discovers the art of 'cattle whispering' when she meets the herd of longhorns that help to conserve the Forest and, at dusk, she's on the trail of one of the UK's rarest creatures. Wildlife film-maker Hamza Yassin gets up close to a much-loved seabird, the puffin, near his home on the west coast of Scotland, while Gareth Wyn Jones offers a glimpse into his life as a hill farmer in Wales. Down on his own farm, Adam Henson has a shearing dilemma to solve.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Cromer seafront in Norfolk. Items brought in to be valued include two pieces of Italian jewellery, a Chinese vase, and a silver goblet that survived the Great Fire of London in perfect condition. Philip Mould examines a pencil sketch by LS Lowry to determine if it is real or a fake.(n)

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: Period mystery set in 19th-century New Zealand at the height of the 1860s gold rush, and based on the Man Booker Prize-winning novel by Eleanor Catton. Politician Alistair Lauderback arrives at a cottage to find a woman, unconscious with opium, a Maori man - also unconscious - and a corpse. Lauderback brings the woman to town, where she is arrested for public intoxication and held at the jail. Nine months earlier, Anna Wetherell arrives in New Zealand. After a romantic first encounter, she agrees to meet with fellow passenger Emery Staines, but scheming fortune-teller Lydia Wells has other ideas. Starring Eva Green, Eve Hewson, Benedict Hardie and Himesh Patel.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the day's three Premier League fixtures, which were Everton v Liverpool at Goodison Park, Aston Villa v Chelsea at Villa Park and Newcastle United v Sheffield United at St James' Park. Jurgen Klopp's Reds were looking to record their third consecutive victory in all competitions against their bitter rivals, and the omens looked good with the Toffees' last triumph coming way back in October 2010. Villa had a similarly poor recent record against Chelsea, having lost their previous five meetings, while the Blades hadn't beaten the Magpies since November 2006.(n)

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Father's Day
programme.desc: Ahead of a Father's Day with a difference his year, the comedian sets up for a day on the road outside Michael's front door. With social distancing taken into account, the two of them will spend some quality time together as best they can, look at some old family photos, awkward home video footage and tell some classic Whitehall tales.(n)

programme.desc: The Roman Catholic priest struggles to save Sid from himself when he vows vengeance on his former defence barrister. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman finds herself in the east Devon countryside with a couple who have £750,000 to find a more pastoral life and business. While in the county, Nicki goes deep into the woods to meet an artist who transforms fallen timber into beautiful bespoke winged sculptures.(n)

programme.desc: Five dealers bid against each other to buy four new items - a vintage bucket with a dark past, a beautiful jewellery box, two chairs that bamboozle them and an antique foot warmer.(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Daniel De Simone investigates a global network of neo-Nazis that aims to destroy society, discovering that it is recruiting in the UK. Police say right-wing extremism is the fastest growing terrorist threat in the UK and that the coronavirus pandemic may be leaving young people vulnerable to radicalisation. As Daniel investigates he notices certain names cropping up again and again and tracks down some of the movement's most influential figures and reveals how the network operates across the globe.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by some of Walford's biggest stars to relive their characters' most iconic moments, share behind-the-scenes stories and reveal stuff that is normally kept under wraps. She begins by catching up with Danny Dyer and Kellie Bright - Mick and Linda Carter - who talk about an embarrassing wardrobe malfunction and give Stacey a tour of the Queen Vic. Danny also shares his best 'doof doof' face, and reveals which EastEnders legend sent him a special note before he took over the pub.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Tom Allen, Geoff Norcott, Vicki Pepperdine and Esme Young join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies.(n)

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: Anna Wetherell is now working at Lydia Wells' fortune parlour, The House of Many Wishes, where she learns that Lydia is plotting with Francis Carver to steal from Crosbie. Carver deceives Emery Staines into thinking that Anna has left for the goldfields. Emery goes to follow her, first agreeing to split his future goldfield winnings with Carver, registering their joint claim under the name 'Aurora'. Emery almost drowns upon his arrival on the Hokitika goldfields, and believing Anna to have drowned as well, he befriends a Maori man named Te Rau Tauwhare, and heads into the hills to grieve.(n)

Politician Alistair Lauderback arrives at a cottage to find a woman, unconscious with opium, a Maori man - also unconscious - and a corpse. Lauderback brings the woman to town, where she is arrested for public intoxication and held at the jail. Nine months earlier, Anna Wetherell arrives in New Zealand. After a romantic first encounter, she agrees to meet with fellow passenger Emery Staines, but scheming fortune-teller Lydia Wells has other ideas. Starring Eva Green, Eve Hewson, Benedict Hardie and Himesh Patel.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the day's three Premier League fixtures, which were Everton v Liverpool at Goodison Park, Aston Villa v Chelsea at Villa Park and Newcastle United v Sheffield United at St James' Park. Jurgen Klopp's Reds were looking to record their third consecutive victory in all competitions against their bitter rivals, and the omens looked good with the Toffees' last triumph coming way back in October 2010. Villa had a similarly poor recent record against Chelsea, having lost their previous five meetings, while the Blades hadn't beaten the Magpies since November 2006.(n)

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Father's Day
programme.desc: Ahead of a Father's Day with a difference his year, the comedian sets up for a day on the road outside Michael's front door. With social distancing taken into account, the two of them will spend some quality time together as best they can, look at some old family photos, awkward home video footage and tell some classic Whitehall tales.(n)

programme.desc: A neighbourly dispute gets out of hand, a shop worker fears for his life when two violent men burst in, and a thief goes into a pet shop and steals a puppy.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Roman Catholic priest struggles to save Sid from himself when he vows vengeance on his former defence barrister. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman finds herself in the east Devon countryside with a couple who have £750,000 to find a more pastoral life and business. While in the county, Nicki goes deep into the woods to meet an artist who transforms fallen timber into beautiful bespoke winged sculptures.(n)

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against each other to buy four new items - a vintage bucket with a dark past, a beautiful jewellery box, two chairs that bamboozle them and an antique foot warmer.(n)
