programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Daniel De Simone investigates a global network of neo-Nazis that aims to destroy society, discovering that it is recruiting in the UK. Police say right-wing extremism is the fastest growing terrorist threat in the UK and that the coronavirus pandemic may be leaving young people vulnerable to radicalisation. As Daniel investigates he notices certain names cropping up again and again and tracks down some of the movement's most influential figures and reveals how the network operates across the globe.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by some of Walford's biggest stars to relive their characters' most iconic moments, share behind-the-scenes stories and reveal stuff that is normally kept under wraps. She begins by catching up with Danny Dyer and Kellie Bright - Mick and Linda Carter - who talk about an embarrassing wardrobe malfunction and give Stacey a tour of the Queen Vic. Danny also shares his best 'doof doof' face, and reveals which EastEnders legend sent him a special note before he took over the pub.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Tom Allen, Geoff Norcott, Vicki Pepperdine and Esme Young join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies.(n)

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: Anna Wetherell is now working at Lydia Wells' fortune parlour, The House of Many Wishes, where she learns that Lydia is plotting with Francis Carver to steal from Crosbie. Carver deceives Emery Staines into thinking that Anna has left for the goldfields. Emery goes to follow her, first agreeing to split his future goldfield winnings with Carver, registering their joint claim under the name 'Aurora'. Emery almost drowns upon his arrival on the Hokitika goldfields, and believing Anna to have drowned as well, he befriends a Maori man named Te Rau Tauwhare, and heads into the hills to grieve.(n)

programme.title: Pretty Single
programme.desc: Law graduate Michaela spends hours perfecting her look for photos on social media, but hopes that going on a series of dates make-up free will pave the way to her perfect match.(n)

programme.title: I May Destroy You
programme.desc: Arabella remembers having sex with Biagio, but wakes up next to a sleeping Zain. Kwame goes to a police station and is interviewed by a well-meaning but out-of-his-depth officer, who can't understand why Kwame doesn't know the accused's name. Arabella goes on stage to do a reading at a literary event, but instead stuns the room by declaring that Zain is a rapist under UK law. As Zain tries to leave, Terry films him on her phone, and the clip goes viral on the internet. Drama, starring Michaela Coel.(n)

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: Jack Whitehall pops by for a chat and a game of Musical VAR, Anne-Marie Saves Our Summer with an exclusive performance in The Garden Sessions and Mrs Crouch - Abbey Clancy - drops in for an extra special edition of Peter's Extreme Retirement.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A classic episode of the soap from 1986 directed by acclaimed film-maker Antonia Bird, which was the first episode of the show to feature only two characters. Den Watts has demanded a divorce from his wife Angie, who resorts to desperate measures to ensure he does not leave her. Starring Anita Dobson and Leslie Grantham.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Fletch's surgery is derailed when Evie reveals a secret that makes him question himself, but both suspect they are just making excuses out of a fear of what the future may hold. Nicky treats a familiar face, and is put in a difficult position by her mother that forces her to choose between cutting her off or making a promise she cannot keep. Max's big day is ruined by devastating revelations from three of her colleagues.(n)

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Talking Heads
programme.desc: New performances of Alan Bennett's monologues. Imelda Staunton performs A Lady of Letters, the story of a woman who compulsively spies on her neighbours. She becomes fascinated by a little girl who lives across the road, who displays some unusual bruises. But when she begins sending aggressive letters to the child's parents, it leads her into trouble with the police.(n)

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Talking Heads
programme.desc: Sarah Lancashire performs Alan Bennett's monologue An Ordinary Woman, the story of a mother who finds herself developing incestuous desires for her own 15-year-old son. She feels unable to confide in her husband and sees herself has too normal to admit to anything so shocking, but things are brought to a head by the revelation that her son has a girlfriend.(n)

programme.desc: The curmudgeonly GP is perturbed when he discovers Louisa, who is already spending much of her time away from home, has applied to take up a child and adolescent counselling course. However, achieving a work-life balance is far from the greatest of concerns for some of the Ellinghams' neighbours, not least of whom is Al, whose plans to demonstrate his business acumen are placed in jeopardy when Ken decides to close the pub. programme.title: Alexander Armstrong's Land of the Midnight Sun
programme.desc: The presenter continues his epic 8,000-mile journey halfway around the Arctic Circle deep in mid-winter. On the third and final leg of his odyssey, he begins in Northern Canada before travelling into the icy wilderness of Alaska. Along the way, Alexander meets people who have headed north to discover adventure and seek their fortune, as well as digging for gold with modern day prospectors. He also takes on the challenge of driving up one of the deadliest roads in the world, before concluding his travels on the international dateline between America and Russia.(n)

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: The comedian talks to actor Matthew Macfadyen about his portrayal of Major Charles Ingram in the ITV drama Quiz. James Bay performs a tribute to singer-songwriter Bill Withers.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: With the pub shut, Chas feels like she is cut off from her old life, while Paddy is keen to get things back on track between them. Before long he has plans for them both, but will Chas allow him to carry her away with his dreams?(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tracy persuades a reluctant Leanne that they should have a family day out at the petting zoo as it would do Oliver good. Daniel heads to a hotel where he meets Nicky, Carla sets about decorating the Rovers for VE Day, and Seb plucks up the courage to tell Alina that he still has feelings for her.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: A selection of home videos sent in by members of the public and presented in a format to entertain people of all ages, narrated by Harry Hill. A camel smashes a car window, a runaway speedboat causes a traffic accident and a drummer is crushed by a crucifix in church.(n)

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: A new corporate vice president shakes things up at the store. Mateo gets involved in relationship rumours and Dina gets revenge against a snitch.(n)

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: During the morning meeting, Glenn accidentally tells the employees the reason Amy has been late to work lately and rumours spread about the state of her marriage. In an attempt to cheer Amy up, Dina organises a ladies' lunch and forces everyone to participate. Comedy, starring America Ferrera.(n)

programme.title: Dinner Date
programme.desc: Stephanie from Leeds picks three blind dates from five potential partners, based entirely on the menus they have created. When the three dinners have been eaten, she decides who she wants to see again. Narrated by Charlotte Hudson.(n)

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Manchester, Jason, Nathan and Jake all hope to impress hotel bookings agent Olivia with their style choices.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: With the pub shut, Chas feels like she is cut off from her old life, while Paddy is keen to get things back on track between them. Before long he has plans for them both, but will Chas allow him to carry her away with his dreams?(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring a party trick gone wrong, a rogue wave interrupting a photo opportunity and a lesson in why balloons should not be used near work tools.(n)

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Ricky Gervais to talk about the return of Netflix comedy After Life. Plus, Icelandic indie band Of Monsters and Men perform.(n)

programme.title: Dinner Date
programme.desc: Martyn from Cardiff picks three blind dates from five potential partners, based entirely on the three course menus they have created. Having dined with the three ladies, he decides who he wants to see again.(n)

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three potential suitors, Brian, Elmo and Michael, compete for the affections of Lauren from Milton Keynes.(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: A model, a gardener, a cage fighter and Mr Luton are the latest candidates to enter the 'love lift' in the hope of impressing 30 single women and winning a date - but must first get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Paddy McGuinness presents.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring clip in which a tiny trip is all it takes to cause a huge catastrophe.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring a vlogger squirting perfume in her eyes and a runaway car wrecking a campsite.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. Highlights include a girl dropping a full spaghetti bolognese over her computer tablet, dashcam footage of a tree almost crushing a car and a selfie being spoiled by a naked photo-bomber's bum.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring a meal interrupted by a fireball, a toddler soaked in llama phlegm, and a teenager knocked over by a runaway tyre.(n)

programme.title: Roswell, New Mexico
programme.desc: On the anniversary of Rosa's death, tensions begin to flare and a shocking revelation is uncovered.(n)

programme.title: Love Island: Australia
programme.desc: Eden and Erin go on a date, Grant gets a wax, the Islanders ride the Truth Bike, and Francoise takes her pick breaking up a couple.(n)

programme.title: Killer Camp
programme.desc: The campmates try to recover from the previous night's shocking killing as their suspicions see bromances broken, alliances forged and a relationship blossom.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin is struggling to live off his student grant, so the girls find a way for him to earn hard cash. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Carla insists that she has no idea who Chelsey is, but is shocked when she reminds Ms Connor that she stayed in her squat when she was sleeping rough last year. Sally refuses to sit with Tim and Geoff in the pub, and Daniel is thrilled when Adam offers to spend his birthday with him and Bertie.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Newcomer Jordan tells Carla she had better pay up for the drugs she destroyed. Adam insists Daniel should go with him to a lawyers' drinks reception in town, and Roy returns from a meeting with Shona's key worker and confirms that David's confused ex will be moving in tomorrow.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Carla confesses to Peter that Jordan is trying to blackmail her. Adam is pleased to see Daniel having a good time with an attractive woman, and Alya tells Imran how she suspects Sally kicked Geoff out because underneath, she knows Yasmeen is innocent. However, Faye shows her support for Geoff.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: With Mandy and Vinny holed up in the salon for lockdown, things are a little less conventional. They have an endless supply of beauty products, some beers, a microwave and a karaoke machine. With Paul the main topic of conversation, Mandy and Vinny navigate through their past and soon Mandy discloses a long-held secret that has affected her whole life. Vinny at last understands her resentments and fears.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: While Nicola and Jimmy wind each other up in lockdown, she is seemingly busy with her local council business, but something is bothering her. Jimmy can't help but be aware of her sadness, but he's at a loss at what to do.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: Peril at End House - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. While holidaying in Cornwall with his friend Captain Hastings, the sleuth meets a local woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play. David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: Peril at End House - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A casino is robbed, presenting Frost with a fresh challenge to his deductive powers, and things go from bad to worse when a local copper is reported missing and a well-known armed thug is spotted in Denton. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and George Anton.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Barnaby launches a murder investigation when the severed hand of a missing hotel manager is found by a dog. The dead man had recently been named in the will of the hotel owner, and solving the case takes on a renewed sense of urgency when fatal accidents start happening to the other beneficiaries. Mystery, guest starring Rosemary Leach and Samantha Bond, with John Nettles and Daniel Casey.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of a journalist has chilling echoes of a 1960s horror film starring Midsomer's own Stella Harris. When the actress's more famous sister Diana returns after a 40-year family rift, the body count rises, with each crime reminiscent of a movie plot - giving Barnaby and Jones what looks like a disturbing case of life imitating art. Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes star, with Harriet Walter and Sinead Cusack.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of her old friend Father Gorman, using a list of names sent by the priest moments before his death to bring the guilty party to justice. Following a trail of clues to London, Marple is drawn to the Pale Horse Inn - where she discovers a sinister world of occult practices. Drama, starring Julia McKenzie in the title role, with Lynda Baron, Pauline Collins, Nicholas Parsons and Neil Pearson.(n)

programme.title: Deep Water
programme.desc: On the school trip, Lisa struggles to get close enough to Kate to ask her what has been going on, and watches in shock as Kate continues to play the role of the tragic mother of a missing daughter. The tension amidst the parents begins to rub off on the children, and a playful accident sees Fergus rushed into hospital. Lisa follows Kate and Fergus to hospital, determined to uncover the truth. Later, Roz is forced to tell Abby that Winston has moved out. Starring Anna Friel, Sinead Keenan and Rosalind Eleazar.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A barmaid's murder leads Lewis (Kevin Whately) and Hathaway (Laurence Fox) to a respected professor, who admits he once had an affair with the woman. However, they discover the killing was copied straight from the pages of a popular fantasy novel written by an Oxford tutor, suggesting it may be the work of a crazed fan - or something even more sinister. Guest starring Art Malik, Tom Mison and James Fox, father of Laurence.(n)

programme.title: The Loch
programme.desc: The police hold a press conference to appeal for information about Jonjo, and Smilie hopes to take the opportunity to reassure the public that they have nothing to fear and prevent news of the human heart found by the lake from spreading further. However, his plans are disrupted by Blake, who reveals everything and shares his theory that the community is being terrorised by a serial killer. An unexpected discovery is made outside the village hall during a town meeting set up to try and identify the killer.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Ed is left in charge of the endangered-species project while Danny is away and Du Plessis is doing his community service. A beautiful stranger turns up at the clinic, claiming to have a lion that carries the rare Barbary gene, and she appears to take a romantic interest in him. However, Du Plessis grows suspicious and fears the newcomer has an ulterior motive. Meanwhile, Fatani struggles while helping Liv and Thabo with the animal census.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack finds himself working alongside DS Lawson on a delicate case involving a spate of attacks on defenceless senior citizens in their homes, while Lawson also offers him some advice on his personal life. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander, Sally Dexter and Neil Phillips.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: With Mandy and Vinny holed up in the salon for lockdown, things are a little less conventional. They have an endless supply of beauty products, some beers, a microwave and a karaoke machine. With Paul the main topic of conversation, Mandy and Vinny navigate through their past and soon Mandy discloses a long-held secret that has affected her whole life. Vinny at last understands her resentments and fears.(n)

programme.desc: While Nicola and Jimmy wind each other up in lockdown, she is seemingly busy with her local council business, but something is bothering her. Jimmy can't help but be aware of her sadness, but he's at a loss at what to do.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Andy accuses Des of betraying him and refuses to believe there is nothing between him and Liz. Tony swaps over to the Rovers' team. Audrey gets tipsy at a Greek restaurant.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz is confused by her feelings for Des and Jim. Steve tells Vicky he feels she is acting like his probation officer. Derek is shocked when Norris asks him to be his best man.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dave and Mandy's date does not go according to Mandy's plans. Jack discovers another cow has been stolen. Tina thinks Frank may have pushed Kim too far.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The bobbies of Aidensfield try to get to the bottom of a series of burglaries in which the culprit only steals certain valuable objects, including Tricia's postcard collection. Meanwhile, Blaketon attempts to raise support for his local election campaign, with surprising results. Rural police drama, starring Jason Durr, Clare Calbraith and Derek Fowlds.(n)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.sub-title: Two Heads Are Better Than One
programme.desc: Mike attempts to earn some cash without Sid's interference. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James.(n)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The family return from holiday, and it seems as if they never went away. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Mr Roper decides he will not allow the flatmates to have a party. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: It is July 1945 and, as Britain settles into peacetime, Foyle looks forward to retirement, but finds his life in turmoil once more when Samantha's employer Sir Leonard Spencer-Jones is found dead. Suspicion falls on Niko, a Russian PoW, who flees the Sussex coast and heads for a sanctuary in London - closely followed by Foyle, who has been sent to track him down by the War Office. But the investigation soon brings to light a conspiracy and an international cover-up which, if exposed, could bring down the British government. Max Brown joins the cast, with Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks, Anthony Howell, Eleanor Bron and Christopher Good.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Paying guests the Langleys arrive for a shooting party at Ashfordly Hall, but their grandiose ideas upset the locals and it is not long before a sinister plot is revealed. Vernon's latest money-making scheme seems destined to end in disaster as David takes the wheel of an old Army vehicle, but his off-road tours are not for the faint-hearted. Nostalgic rural drama, guest starring Robert Glenister and Celia Imrie.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy is devastated after her disastrous date with Dave. Robert insists the Dingles stole the cow but Jack is unconvinced. Scott is still being menaced by Marcus.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles do not know where the cow has gone. Eric plans to host a singles night at the wine bar. Linda confides in Zoe about her problems.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz tells Des she knows Sean wants to get rid of her. Curly goes into work and breaks the news of his marriage. Rita and Mavis return from their cruise.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim is amazed when Liz tells him she has been laid off by Des. The Firmans entertain Curly and Raquel. Vicky buys Steve a personalised number plate for Christmas.(n)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid's new neighbours are offended by Mike's latest sculpture. Comedy, starring Sid James.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Paying guests the Langleys arrive for a shooting party at Ashfordly Hall, but their grandiose ideas upset the locals and it is not long before a sinister plot is revealed. Vernon's latest money-making scheme seems destined to end in disaster as David takes the wheel of an old Army vehicle, but his off-road tours are not for the faint-hearted. Nostalgic rural drama, guest starring Robert Glenister and Celia Imrie.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Harriet Walter (Law & Order: UK) joins the cast as the costume drama returns. Following Matthew's shocking death, new mother Mary is struggling to come to terms with her loss, and Robert and Violet disagree over how to handle the situation. Downstairs, Jimmy finally takes an interest in Ivy, while Carson receives an unwelcome reminder of his past. Mrs Hughes sees a chance to help grief-stricken Isobel, and Thomas has the children's nanny in his sights. Michelle Dockery, Hugh Bonneville, Maggie Smith, Ed Speleers and Phyllis Logan also star.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective drama prequel takes inspiration from an episode from Oxford's real-life history, in which Nation of Islam preacher and civil rights campaigner Marcus X was invited to speak at the Oxford Union in December 1964, less than two months before his assassination in New York. Here, Morse struggles to negotiate escalating social tensions as a protest at a hair salon exposes rapidly escalating racial unrest in the heart of the city. Meanwhile, a murder at an Army base threatens to unveil long-held secrets within the regiment. Guest starring Marcus Griffiths, with Shaun Evans and Roger Allam.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Racial tensions erupt in Hastings when a black GI is suspected of murdering a local woman. The victim, an ex-lover of the suspect and the mother of his baby, was staying at a guest house where Samantha is assistant manager, and had been disowned by her family because of her mixed-race child. As Foyle investigates, he is not convinced the soldier could be capable of such a crime, and soon comes up against the might of the US Army as he tries to crack the case. Starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks, Obi Abili and Charlotte Riley.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A confrontation between hunt stewards and saboteurs leads to the suspicious death of a protester - and makes Jack wonder whether the thrill of the chase has given the horsey set a taste for human blood. Whodunit, starring David Jason as DI Frost, with Bruce Alexander, Sean Pertwee, Del Henney and Tat Whalley.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A murder gives rise to rumours of a communist conspiracy. Poirot becomes the centre of attention at the Chelsea Flower Show as he views a rose named in his honour, while Hastings receives a packet of seeds that give him hay fever. Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The age gap between Sid and his children becomes apparent when he returns home early from work. Comedy, starring Sid James.(n)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid arranges a fishing weekend, blissfully unaware Jean's father is coming to stay. Classic family comedy, starring Sid James.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: When the trio decide they need a fourth person to share the flat, Robin is determined it will be a woman. Seventies comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A father is aghast to read tales of wild romance in his teenage daughter's diary, failing to realise they are all fictitious. David inherits a ventriloquist's dummy complete with a schedule of bookings, and decides the show must go on. Meanwhile, Gina finds herself attracted to a charismatic guest. Nostalgic drama, starring David Lonsdale and Tricia Penrose.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Linda and Biff start the day with an argument. Eric encourages Seth to come to the singles night. Scott is shocked when Marcus shows him he has a gun.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel is upset that she let Curly down. Steve enjoys giving out bottles of whisky as Christmas presents. Jim cannot understand why Liz will not let him anywhere near Des.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera has an exhausting Christmas running the Rovers. Curly and Raquel have some explaining to do. Vicky despairs of Steve.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A horse whisperer disappears in Newton Magna, bringing Barnaby face to face with long-standing adversary Melvyn Stockard. The former convict's social climbing has paid dividends, sending him straight to the top of the village pecking order - and some believe his driving ambition makes him capable of foul play. Murder mystery, guest starring Larry Lamb (EastEnders, Gavin & Stacey), with John Nettles and Daniel Casey.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A father is aghast to read tales of wild romance in his teenage daughter's diary, failing to realise they are all fictitious. David inherits a ventriloquist's dummy complete with a schedule of bookings, and decides the show must go on. Meanwhile, Gina finds herself attracted to a charismatic guest. Nostalgic drama, starring David Lonsdale and Tricia Penrose.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Mary begins to emerge from her grief, and takes an interest in the running of the estate. However, her ambitions meet firm resistance from her father. Lady Rose MacClare, Lady Flintshire's daughter, finds herself in a predicament at a tea dance in York, while Mrs Hughes urges Carson to heal an old wound. Bates comes up with a plan to help Molesley, but will it work? Costume drama, starring Michelle Dockery, Hugh Bonneville, Lily James, Phyllis Logan, Jim Carter, Brendan Coyle and Kevin Doyle.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Coma victim Harriet Farmer finally wakes up a year after she was attacked while trying to elope, but her boyfriend Finn Robson is still missing. Soon after she regains consciousness, members of a chess club are targeted by a killer. Could the assault that left Harriet for dead be linked to moves on a games board? Guest starring Fay Ripley (Cold Feet), with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle prepares to be replaced at the police station and begin retirement, his attention is grabbed by a news story about a man sentenced to hang for high treason, and he decides to take on the case. But with the prisoner's execution date approaching, the detective has to work fast to find evidence that will clear him. Meanwhile, Samantha and Adam fight to save Hill House from the town planners determined to demolish it - but disaster soon strikes. Starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Max Brown.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of a journalist has chilling echoes of a 1960s horror film starring Midsomer's own Stella Harris. When the actress's more famous sister Diana returns after a 40-year family rift, the body count rises, with each crime reminiscent of a movie plot - giving Barnaby and Jones what looks like a disturbing case of life imitating art. Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes star, with Harriet Walter and Sinead Cusack.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of her old friend Father Gorman, using a list of names sent by the priest moments before his death to bring the guilty party to justice. Following a trail of clues to London, Marple is drawn to the Pale Horse Inn - where she discovers a sinister world of occult practices. Drama, starring Julia McKenzie in the title role, with Lynda Baron, Pauline Collins, Nicholas Parsons and Neil Pearson.(n)

programme.title: Deep Water
programme.desc: On the school trip, Lisa struggles to get close enough to Kate to ask her what has been going on, and watches in shock as Kate continues to play the role of the tragic mother of a missing daughter. The tension amidst the parents begins to rub off on the children, and a playful accident sees Fergus rushed into hospital. Lisa follows Kate and Fergus to hospital, determined to uncover the truth. Later, Roz is forced to tell Abby that Winston has moved out. Starring Anna Friel, Sinead Keenan and Rosalind Eleazar.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A barmaid's murder leads Lewis (Kevin Whately) and Hathaway (Laurence Fox) to a respected professor, who admits he once had an affair with the woman. However, they discover the killing was copied straight from the pages of a popular fantasy novel written by an Oxford tutor, suggesting it may be the work of a crazed fan - or something even more sinister. Guest starring Art Malik, Tom Mison and James Fox, father of Laurence.(n)

programme.title: The Loch
programme.desc: The police hold a press conference to appeal for information about Jonjo, and Smilie hopes to take the opportunity to reassure the public that they have nothing to fear and prevent news of the human heart found by the lake from spreading further. However, his plans are disrupted by Blake, who reveals everything and shares his theory that the community is being terrorised by a serial killer. An unexpected discovery is made outside the village hall during a town meeting set up to try and identify the killer.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Ed is left in charge of the endangered-species project while Danny is away and Du Plessis is doing his community service. A beautiful stranger turns up at the clinic, claiming to have a lion that carries the rare Barbary gene, and she appears to take a romantic interest in him. However, Du Plessis grows suspicious and fears the newcomer has an ulterior motive. Meanwhile, Fatani struggles while helping Liv and Thabo with the animal census.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack finds himself working alongside DS Lawson on a delicate case involving a spate of attacks on defenceless senior citizens in their homes, while Lawson also offers him some advice on his personal life. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander, Sally Dexter and Neil Phillips.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: With Mandy and Vinny holed up in the salon for lockdown, things are a little less conventional. They have an endless supply of beauty products, some beers, a microwave and a karaoke machine. With Paul the main topic of conversation, Mandy and Vinny navigate through their past and soon Mandy discloses a long-held secret that has affected her whole life. Vinny at last understands her resentments and fears.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: While Nicola and Jimmy wind each other up in lockdown, she is seemingly busy with her local council business, but something is bothering her. Jimmy can't help but be aware of her sadness, but he's at a loss at what to do.(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Andy accuses Des of betraying him and refuses to believe there is nothing between him and Liz. Tony swaps over to the Rovers' team. Audrey gets tipsy at a Greek restaurant.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz is confused by her feelings for Des and Jim. Steve tells Vicky he feels she is acting like his probation officer. Derek is shocked when Norris asks him to be his best man.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dave and Mandy's date does not go according to Mandy's plans. Jack discovers another cow has been stolen. Tina thinks Frank may have pushed Kim too far.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The bobbies of Aidensfield try to get to the bottom of a series of burglaries in which the culprit only steals certain valuable objects, including Tricia's postcard collection. Meanwhile, Blaketon attempts to raise support for his local election campaign, with surprising results. Rural police drama, starring Jason Durr, Clare Calbraith and Derek Fowlds.(n)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.sub-title: Two Heads Are Better Than One
programme.desc: Mike attempts to earn some cash without Sid's interference. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James.(n)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The family return from holiday, and it seems as if they never went away. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Mr Roper decides he will not allow the flatmates to have a party. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: It is July 1945 and, as Britain settles into peacetime, Foyle looks forward to retirement, but finds his life in turmoil once more when Samantha's employer Sir Leonard Spencer-Jones is found dead. Suspicion falls on Niko, a Russian PoW, who flees the Sussex coast and heads for a sanctuary in London - closely followed by Foyle, who has been sent to track him down by the War Office. But the investigation soon brings to light a conspiracy and an international cover-up which, if exposed, could bring down the British government. Max Brown joins the cast, with Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks, Anthony Howell, Eleanor Bron and Christopher Good.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Paying guests the Langleys arrive for a shooting party at Ashfordly Hall, but their grandiose ideas upset the locals and it is not long before a sinister plot is revealed. Vernon's latest money-making scheme seems destined to end in disaster as David takes the wheel of an old Army vehicle, but his off-road tours are not for the faint-hearted. Nostalgic rural drama, guest starring Robert Glenister and Celia Imrie.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy is devastated after her disastrous date with Dave. Robert insists the Dingles stole the cow but Jack is unconvinced. Scott is still being menaced by Marcus.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles do not know where the cow has gone. Eric plans to host a singles night at the wine bar. Linda confides in Zoe about her problems.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz tells Des she knows Sean wants to get rid of her. Curly goes into work and breaks the news of his marriage. Rita and Mavis return from their cruise.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim is amazed when Liz tells him she has been laid off by Des. The Firmans entertain Curly and Raquel. Vicky buys Steve a personalised number plate for Christmas.(n)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid's new neighbours are offended by Mike's latest sculpture. Comedy, starring Sid James.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Paying guests the Langleys arrive for a shooting party at Ashfordly Hall, but their grandiose ideas upset the locals and it is not long before a sinister plot is revealed. Nostalgic rural drama, guest starring Robert Glenister and Celia Imrie.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Harriet Walter (Law & Order: UK) joins the cast as the costume drama returns. Following Matthew's shocking death, new mother Mary is struggling to come to terms with her loss, and Robert and Violet disagree over how to handle the situation. Downstairs, Jimmy finally takes an interest in Ivy, while Carson receives an unwelcome reminder of his past. Mrs Hughes sees a chance to help grief-stricken Isobel, and Thomas has the children's nanny in his sights. Michelle Dockery, Hugh Bonneville, Maggie Smith, Ed Speleers and Phyllis Logan also star.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective drama prequel takes inspiration from an episode from Oxford's real-life history, in which Nation of Islam preacher and civil rights campaigner Marcus X was invited to speak at the Oxford Union in December 1964, less than two months before his assassination in New York. Here, Morse struggles to negotiate escalating social tensions as a protest at a hair salon exposes rapidly escalating racial unrest in the heart of the city. Meanwhile, a murder at an Army base threatens to unveil long-held secrets within the regiment. Guest starring Marcus Griffiths, with Shaun Evans and Roger Allam.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Racial tensions erupt in Hastings when a black GI is suspected of murdering a local woman. The victim, an ex-lover of the suspect and the mother of his baby, was staying at a guest house where Samantha is assistant manager, and had been disowned by her family because of her mixed-race child. As Foyle investigates, he is not convinced the soldier could be capable of such a crime, and soon comes up against the might of the US Army as he tries to crack the case. Starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks, Obi Abili and Charlotte Riley.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A confrontation between hunt stewards and saboteurs leads to the suspicious death of a protester - and makes Jack wonder whether the thrill of the chase has given the horsey set a taste for human blood. Whodunit, starring David Jason as DI Frost, with Bruce Alexander, Sean Pertwee, Del Henney and Tat Whalley.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A murder gives rise to rumours of a communist conspiracy. Poirot becomes the centre of attention at the Chelsea Flower Show as he views a rose named in his honour, while Hastings receives a packet of seeds that give him hay fever. Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A father is aghast to read tales of wild romance in his teenage daughter's diary, failing to realise they are all fictitious. David inherits a ventriloquist's dummy complete with a schedule of bookings, and decides the show must go on. Meanwhile, Gina finds herself attracted to a charismatic guest. Nostalgic drama, starring David Lonsdale and Tricia Penrose.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera has an exhausting Christmas running the Rovers. Curly and Raquel have some explaining to do. Vicky despairs of Steve.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A horse whisperer disappears in Newton Magna, bringing Barnaby face to face with long-standing adversary Melvyn Stockard. The former convict's social climbing has paid dividends, sending him straight to the top of the village pecking order - and some believe his driving ambition makes him capable of foul play. Murder mystery, guest starring Larry Lamb (EastEnders, Gavin & Stacey), with John Nettles and Daniel Casey.(n)

programme.desc: Mary begins to emerge from her grief, and takes an interest in the running of the estate. However, her ambitions meet firm resistance from her father. Lady Rose MacClare, Lady Flintshire's daughter, finds herself in a predicament at a tea dance in York, while Mrs Hughes urges Carson to heal an old wound. Bates comes up with a plan to help Molesley, but will it work? Costume drama, starring Michelle Dockery, Hugh Bonneville, Lily James, Phyllis Logan, Jim Carter, Brendan Coyle and Kevin Doyle.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Coma victim Harriet Farmer finally wakes up a year after she was attacked while trying to elope, but her boyfriend Finn Robson is still missing. Soon after she regains consciousness, members of a chess club are targeted by a killer. Could the assault that left Harriet for dead be linked to moves on a games board? Guest starring Fay Ripley (Cold Feet), with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle prepares to be replaced at the police station and begin retirement, his attention is grabbed by a news story about a man sentenced to hang for high treason, and he decides to take on the case. But with the prisoner's execution date approaching, the detective has to work fast to find evidence that will clear him. Meanwhile, Samantha and Adam fight to save Hill House from the town planners determined to demolish it - but disaster soon strikes. Starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Max Brown.(n)

programme.desc: Some of the best strikers in the history of the football World Cup.(n)

programme.title: The Unrideables 2: The Rainey Days
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the career of American motorcycling star Wayne Rainey, who was one of the dominant riders in the sport in the 1980s and 1990s. Featuring footage of Rainey battling against the likes of Mick Doohan, Kevin Schwantz and Eddie Lawson, along with contributions from journalists, managers and riders.(n)

programme.title: Steve McQueen: Motorcycle Movie Star
programme.desc: In the second of two programmes, Henry Cole delves further into McQueen's turbulent life and the battles he had with Hollywood. He talks bikes with US TV chat show host Jay Leno, rides McQueen's favourite Indian motorcycle and uncovers the secrets behind his famous film, The Great Escape.(n)

programme.title: Return of the Seven (1966)
programme.desc: The leader of a gang of bandits enslaves all the men from a Mexican village while maddened with grief over the death of his son. However, one of his hostages is a survivor from the original team of gunfighters, and his wife persuades his two remaining companions to form a new band of heroes and mount a rescue mission. Western sequel, starring Yul Brynner, Robert Fuller and Warren Oates.(n)

programme.title: Snooker: Tour Championship Live
programme.desc: Coverage of day two from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, featuring the first eight frames of a quarter-final, played over the best of 17 frames. Last year, Judd Trump overcame Mark Williams in a tight encounter by a 9-8 scoreline, while Ronnie O'Sullivan made lighter work of Stuart Bingham, triumphing 9-3. The Rocket will not be involved this year, however, having failed to qualify. Jill Douglas presents, with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Neal Foulds, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates.(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean

programme.title: Mr Bean

programme.title: British Touring Cars

This bonus went to Ronnie O'Sullivan last year, but The Rocket's failure to qualify for this year's tournament means another player will be in line to scoop the cash. programme.desc: A spoof of TV thriller Homeland in which Homer returns from a nuclear convention a changed man. Lisa's suspicions are aroused when she realises her dad has given up pork and beer and has developed a sudden fondness for power plant schematics, so she enlists the services of an unhinged FBI agent (voiced by Kristen Wiig, Bridesmaids) to get to the bottom of his behaviour.(n)

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester.(n)

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather.(n)

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: High up on Dartmoor, shepherds Steve and Crispin Alford gather their sheep for a speed-shearing competition. It's a big day for Steve, as it's the first time he's hosted the annual charity event on his farm. In east Devon, master wheelwrights Greg and Mike Rowland embark on a new commission - building a gun carriage to sit aboard the HMS Warrior, while on the south coast of Cornwall, foraging chef Greg Milne wanders the woodland in search of inspiration for a new dish to serve at his restaurant on St Michael's Mount.(n)

programme.title: Murder in the Car Park
programme.desc: The docudrama about the inquiry into the killing of private investigator Daniel Morgan continues. This episode examines how tabloid scandals - which eventually drew senior members of the government into a large public enquiry into the ethics of the press - had connections to the original suspects in the Morgan murder. For the first time, those who were alleged to have committed the crime, the police officers who investigated, and the grieving brother of the victim, all tell their side of the story.(n)

programme.title: The Battle for Britain's Heroes
programme.desc: Writer Afua Hirsch believes the United Kingdom needs to question some of the uncomfortable aspects of its beloved heroes. She is met with opposition as she confronts Horatio Nelson's pro-slavery views and Winston Churchill's opinions on race. Afua also visits Germany to see if the UK can learn from how they have addressed their past.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The hospital documentary returns, focusing on the stories of two motorcyclists, which reveal how the bonds between loved ones can be strengthened through adversity. Prezmek may have life-changing spinal injuries following a high-speed motorcycle crash on the M4, forcing his wife Tatia to reflect on how their plans for the future may have to change. Teenager Leon undergoes a CT scan after crashing into a central reservation. While waiting for the results, his mother Janey thinks back to his school days.(n)

programme.title: 50 Shades of Gay
programme.desc: Rupert Everett explores the changes in gay life in the 50 years since the decriminalisation of male homosexuality in England and Wales in 1967. He meets LGBT people from all walks of life, including former royal butler Paul Burrell, rapper Jai'Rouge, actors from Hollyoaks, and Essex carpenter Steve, and visits Hebden Bridge in West Yorkshire, known as the lesbian capital of Britain.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Fiery chef Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of the owners of Hannah & Mason's, a French bistro in Cranbury, New Jersey. Members of staff complain their pay cheques bounce, and the kitchen cooler is so unhygienic that Gordon makes the decision to close the restaurant on Valentine's Day - the busiest night of the year.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three pairs of partners in Lancashire take part in the dinner party competition, hoping to secure the £1,000 prize. First to host are Rosie and her husband David, who have been married for 25 years and are seriously loved-up. Next, young newlyweds Melisa and Kyle cook together for the first time to mixed reviews. The third and final evening is led by bickering couple Gary and Linda, but the duo may need to work hard if they want to be in with a chance of winning the cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Liam Charles and Tom Allen welcome six new teams into the tent, and challenge them to prepare 24 perfectly uniform miniature classics - tarte chiboust vallée d'Auge and rice puddings. In the second round, they put their own spin on Florida's famous key lime pie, which must be decorated with towering edible sculptures. Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden assess the results and decide who will be going on to the next round.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie: Keep Crafting and Carry On
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp uses a clever dying method to create the winter panel for her patchwork cushion, decorates dishes and builds model animals from leftover toilet roll inner tubes.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Best Parent?
programme.desc: Anita Rani invites the audience to choose between three more sets of parents to decide which couple is raising their children in the best way. Vegans Mark and Simone believe that bringing their five children up on a plant-based diet not only makes them healthier but makes them take responsibility for the planet. They are up against Sophie and Phil - who believe that teaching their two children to be competitive prepares them for the working world - and Jen and Tom, who are teaching their kids about the great outdoors, including lighting fires and whittling sticks.(n)

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking and Carry On
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver shares easy recipes for jumbo fish fingers, sweet potato wedges and chopped salad, then digs into the archive for a tasty aubergine daal and simple chickpea chard pork.(n)

programme.title: Cheers

programme.desc: Penny feels threatened when a sexy actress, who has landed a role in TV series CSI, moves into the apartment building, and gradually begins to sense that her friends are being manipulated by the new arrival. Comedy, starring Kaley Cuoco, Valerie Azlynn, Johnny Galecki and Simon Helberg.(n)

programme.desc: As the advancing years begin to take their toll on Frasier, he wonders whether a relationship with a 20-year-old sales assistant would help him recapture some of his youthful vitality. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer.(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Cranes set off on a road trip in a motor home, but Martin complicates matters by inadvertently driving into Canada - which means they have to try to sneak Daphne back across the border due to the terms of her American visa. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer, John Mahoney and Jane Leeves.(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles and Frasier decide to collaborate on a self-help book about sibling relationships. After struggling to write it they lock themselves in a hotel room until it is finished, but petty jealousy reigns and the Crane brothers are soon at each other's throats.(n)

programme.desc: The competition heads to Manchester, where battling it out for the £1,000 cash prize are part-time DJ Dom, 35-year old barber Danny, trainee barrister Sasha, nurse Katherine and goth Maria. In the first edition, DJ Dom kicks off the week serving up a menu inspired by the 1990s 'Madchester' music scene.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Trainee barrister Sasha hosts the second night in Manchester, where she hopes to impress her guests with a Chinese-Pakistani fusion menu. Meanwhile, fellow contestant Maria reveals more about her love of gothic culture, and Danny discusses his soon-to-be-written autobiography.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nurse Katherine hosts the third dinner party in Manchester, and she opts for a simple menu of a smoked salmon with horseradish cream starter, and a chorizo and chickpea main course. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the final night in Manchester and glamorous goth Maria is hoping to wow her guests with her twist on Asian cuisine. However, an avant-garde transgender dancer creates a stir, and at one point it seems possible that Maria has bitten off more than she can chew.(n)

programme.title: Supermarket Wars: Battle of the High Street
programme.desc: How retail giants have fought to win control of a multi-billion-pound industry, following the bitter battles that have taken place over the decades, starting in Victorian times as the likes of Tesco, Waitrose and Sainsbury's emerged. From the introduction of barcodes in the 1980s, to the ad wars that took place on TV screens in the 2000s, the programme reveals how the biggest brands have used every trick in the retail book to try to dominate the competition, but that none of the home-grown giants knew quite what to expect with the arrival of German competitors, Aldi and Lidl.(n)

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: In Northumbria, a specialist sewage blockage team tackle a series of weather-induced problems, including a manhole spouting wet wipes, a drain blocked by plastic bottles and a poo 'sausage' threatening to send raw sewage back into people's homes. Meanwhile, in Worcestershire, the food inspectors are out in force, conducting a second round of surprise visits on two restaurants that did not quite make the mark last time. With more than 500 deaths a year from food poisoning and more than 7,000 food establishments failing basic hygiene standards annually, the two eateries have their work cut out to satisfy the inspectors.(n)

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: In Dorset, property developer Claire Donovan and her family swap homes and lives with warehouse worker Jason White and his clan. programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in Blackpool

Will an incoming hen party be happy with his cheap-and-cheerful approach? programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Bill Nighy narrates a train journey across Sri Lanka along one of the world's best-preserved Victorian railways. The trip begins in the coastal city of Colombo and moves on to the country's cultural and spiritual heart in Kandy. The journey continues through a a very English town that produces some of the world's finest teas, coming to a stop at a hill station surrounded by tropical forests close to one of the world's most spectacular viaducts - the Nine Arch Bridge.(n)

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.sub-title: Hoarders: Landfill in My Living Room
programme.desc: Documentary following people with a compulsion to hoard. The first episode features a woman diagnosed with cancer whose home is full of rotten food and dead mice, a father whose relationship with his daughter has suffered as a result of his obsession, and a Huddersfield man whose clutter caught fire, burning his house to the ground.(n)

programme.title: Big Body Squad

The documentary looks back at the police investigation as detectives retrace the teenager's last steps, and reveals how CCTV proved crucial in solving the crime.(n)

programme.title: Borderline
programme.desc: The staff are on edge as London Met Detective Terry Bradshaw alerts them that a criminal will be passing through passport control. When all seems to be going wrong, Clive attempts to save the day. Spoof documentary, narrated by Ralf Little.(n)

She and her fellow passengers stop off in Nassau in the Bahamas, where Jane finds solace in the state's oldest Baptist church, and St Martin, where she seeks an intimate underwater audience with a host of sharks. Steve and Ben are in west London to see a tenant who owes £14,000 in unpaid rent, but when they gain access to the property they discover the house has divided up like a hotel and is being sublet. In Sussex, Brian and Del chase a debt of over £10,000 owed to a van rental business for damaged vehicles, before heading to Kent to try to reclaim £6,000 of unpaid rent from a woman financially dependent on a new partner after a disastrous divorce.(n)

Thriller, starring Amber Goldfarb and Damon Runyan.(n)

programme.title: Friends

programme.title: 5 News Tonight

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Graeme Hall continues to find solutions for dogs with problems. After a break-up, Hampshire woman Rebecca leaned on her Manchester terrier Marney for support. But now Marney will not let anyone new into her owner's life. In London, Jasper and Alexis cannot live a normal life because their Irish terrier Albert guards the front door like a bouncer.(n)

programme.title: This Week on the Farm
programme.desc: At Cannon Hall farm, Rob and Dave prepare their bull for mating and relive their childhood by camping out under the stars. A vet pays a visit to a rescue farm near Dewsbury to attend to a boar that keeps trying to escape, while the owners of the real Downton Abbey wait to see if they can expect a new generation of piglets. Plus, a look at how farming is changing under lockdown, including a vegetable supplier's innovative search for new customers.(n)

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben Fogle is reunited with seafarer Rick, who has moved on to pastures new since their original encounter. When Rick last appeared on the series, he was living off the coast of French Polynesia, but he has since made a fresh start on the Pacific island of Fiji. In the intervening years, Rick has acquired a new boat and is now travelling alone - but remains as enthusiastic as ever about his unusual lifestyle and believes that anyone can follow in his wake and gain a newfound sense of freedom - if they have the right attitude.(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris Tarrant travels across Europe to examine the train's role in the First World War. In Northumberland, he enjoys a ride on a preserved locomotive to find out how important the railways were to Britain for mobilisation of troops and the operation of large training camps around the country. Aboard a holiday train commandeered to transport troops and ammunition, Chris tells the stories of two opposing soldiers, one English and one German, who both took trains to the Somme in 1916.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Nazi Mega Bunker
programme.desc: A look at the extraordinary defences and kill zones that surrounded Hitler's bunker in Berlin, a subterranean safehouse protected by 13-foot-thick walls.(n)

programme.title: Back to the Future (1985)
programme.desc: Teenager Marty McFly travels back to the 1950s in a time machine built by his eccentric inventor friend. The return journey proves tricky enough, but he also accidentally alters the sequence of events that led to his parents falling in love - and has to persuade his future dad to win his mother's heart, or he will never be born. Sci-fi comedy adventure, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Lea Thompson and Crispin Glover.(n)

programme.title: The Longest Ride (2015)
programme.desc: A rodeo rider and a college student fall in love, but begin to question whether they have a future together as their dreams lead them in different directions. They form an unexpected friendship with an old man, whose decades-long tale of love provides them with fresh inspiration. Romantic drama based on the novel by Nicholas Sparks, starring Britt Robertson, Scott Eastwood and Alan Alda.(n)

programme.title: The Core (2003)
programme.desc: A chain of bizarre and deadly incidents in several countries leads scientists to the conclusion that the Earth's core has stopped spinning. As chaos erupts around the world, experts hatch a plan to halt the destruction and save the human race - but it involves venturing deep below the planet's surface to detonate a nuclear bomb. Sci-fi thriller, starring Aaron Eckhart, Hilary Swank, Stanley Tucci and DJ Qualls.(n)

programme.title: The Imitation Game (2014)
programme.desc: Biopic of mathematician Alan Turing, whose work during the Second World War translated seemingly unbreakable Nazi codes as well as pioneering the development of computer technology. In the years following the war, his life would be destroyed as his homosexuality was discovered, leaving him facing criminal charges. Starring Benedict Cumberbatch, Keira Knightley and Charles Dance.(n)

programme.title: Jack Reacher: Never Go Back (2016)
programme.desc: The vigilante tries to save an army major who has been framed for murder and treason. He discovers that she has been targeted by a secret government conspiracy, and together they go on the run to expose the truth. Action thriller sequel based on Lee Child's novel, starring Tom Cruise, Cobie Smulders and Danika Yarosh.(n)

programme.title: Plastic (2014)
programme.desc: A gang of credit card thieves accidentally steals from a gangster, who demands to be paid back 10 times over. The leader of the group comes up with a plan to carry out a daring jewellery heist in Miami, but his scheme is undermined as his team begin to turn on each other. Fact-based crime thriller, starring Ed Speleers, Will Poulter and Alfie Allen.(n)

programme.title: Hector and the Search for Happiness (2014)
programme.desc: A psychiatrist is troubled by his lack of personal experience, and feels he does not know enough about life to help his patients. He decides to abandon his dull routine and go on a journey around the world in the hope of discovering the secret of perfect happiness. Comedy based on Francois Lelord's novel, starring Simon Pegg, Rosamund Pike, Toni Collette and Christopher Plummer.(n)

programme.title: BSL Zone
programme.desc: A series of half hour programmes aimed at reflecting the interests, needs and aspirations of the sign language community.(n)

programme.title: The Court-Martial of Billy Mitchell (1955)
programme.desc: In the 1920s, an American colonel accuses the war department of criminal negligence for denying the air force the funding it needs - but the powers-that-be still regard aircraft as an irrelevant part of the military, and respond by putting him on trial. Courtroom drama, starring Gary Cooper and Rod Steiger, with Ralph Bellamy and Elizabeth Montgomery.(n)

programme.title: The Gunfight at Dodge City (1959)
programme.desc: A gambler becomes the sheriff of a frontier town, planning to use his position to avenge his brother's murderers - but the arrival of a face from the past muddies the water, and leaves him doubting the identity of the killer. Western, starring Joel McCrea, Julie Adams, John McIntire, Nancy Gates and Richard Anderson.(n)

programme.title: The Day Will Dawn (1942)
programme.desc: A foreign correspondent working in occupied Norway is assigned to an RAF detail helping freedom fighters to sabotage an important German U-boat installation. He enlists the help of a local sea captain's daughter, but the plan goes wrong and he ends up in enemy hands, facing death by firing squad. Second World War adventure, with Hugh Williams, Deborah Kerr and Ralph Richardson.(n)

programme.title: Film4 Interview Programmes
programme.desc: Stars and directors talk about the experience of making movies in this compendium of personal and practical reflections.(n)

programme.title: Support Your Local Gunfighter (1971)
programme.desc: A slick conman tries to pass his dim-witted sidekick off as a notorious gunslinger as a way of profiting from a dispute between rival companies in a small mining town. Comedy Western, starring James Garner, Jack Elam, Suzanne Pleshette, Harry Morgan and Chuck Connors.(n)

programme.title: Ever After: A Cinderella Story (1998)
programme.desc: A girl grows up in 16th-century Europe under the iron fist of her stepmother, until the love of handsome Prince Henry offers a glimmer of hope. Unfortunately, the royal gent has worries of his own in the shape of an arranged marriage. Luckily, Renaissance genius Leonardo da Vinci is on hand to play fairy godfather and bring the pair together. Romantic drama, starring Drew Barrymore, Anjelica Huston, Dougray Scott and Timothy West.(n)

programme.title: Call Me by Your Name (2017)
programme.desc: Seventeen-year-old Elio begins a relationship with Oliver, his father's research assistant in northern Italy. Oscar and Bafta-winning romantic drama, starring Armie Hammer, Timothee Chalamet and Michael Stuhlbarg.(n)

programme.title: Sing Street (2016)
programme.desc: A teenager in 1980s Dublin suffers a troubled life, having to contend with money troubles, his parents' strained marriage, his drop-out older brother and a tough new school. He forms a band hoping to find an escape route, and resorts to criminal measures to shoot music videos. Comedy drama, starring Ferdia Walsh-Peelo, Aidan Gillen and Maria Doyle Kennedy.(n)

programme.title: In Between (2016)
programme.desc: Drama following three twentysomething Palestinian women from different religious backgrounds who share an apartment in Tel Aviv and are trying to find a balance between traditional and modern culture. Drama, starring Mouna Hawa, Sana Jammelieh and Shaden Kanboura. In Arabic and Hebrew.(n)

programme.title: Sea of Sand (1958)
programme.desc: Allied troops fighting in North Africa in 1943 are redeployed as saboteurs behind enemy lines to blow up Rommel's desert fuel dumps. A clash of personalities between officers initially causes problems, but the men overcome their differences to work together. Second World War adventure, starring Richard Attenborough, John Gregson, Michael Craig and Vincent Ball.(n)

programme.title: Hatari! (1962)
programme.desc: A group of reckless animal trappers travels through the wilds of Africa, but the arrival of a beautiful photographer looks set to cause upheaval among the group. Comedy adventure, starring John Wayne, Red Buttons, Hardy Kruger, Elsa Martinelli and Michele Girardon.(n)

programme.title: Sink the Bismarck! (1960)
programme.desc: Fact-based Second World War drama, charting the mission to track down and eliminate the pride of the German fleet, a fearsome battleship devastating British vessels in the North Atlantic, orchestrated by a British naval officer recovering from his wife's death in an air raid. Starring Kenneth More, Dana Wynter, Laurence Naismith and Karel Stepanek.(n)

programme.title: Mud (2012)
programme.desc: Two boys exploring an island on the Mississippi discover a fugitive has made his home there. He tells them he is on the run from bounty hunters after killing a man, and needs their help to be reunited with his lost love. As they ferry messages to the woman in question, the youngsters are forced to come to terms with the complexity of adult relationships. Drama, starring Matthew McConaughey and Reese Witherspoon.(n)

programme.title: Atomic Blonde (2017)
programme.desc: MI6 operative Lorraine Broughton is sent to Berlin before the fall of the wall to investigate the murder of a fellow spy and recover a missing list of double agents. The city is in a dangerously unstable state, and she finds herself targetted by multiple factions plotting to exploit the uncertain future for their own ruthless ends. Thriller, starring Charlize Theron, James McAvoy and John Goodman.(n)

programme.title: Carol (2015)
programme.desc: A housewife trapped in a loveless marriage in 1950s New York forms a close attachment with a younger woman seeking fulfilment in her own life. A passionate secret affair develops, but as her husband grows suspicious, she finds her competence as a parent being called into question. Romantic drama based on Patricia Highsmith's novel The Price of Salt, starring Cate Blanchett and Rooney Mara.(n)

programme.title: Hollyoaks

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

When she opened Cafe Hon in 1992 it was a roaring success, but as a result of Denise's unwavering business ambition, people started to boycott the restaurant, leaving her frustrated by falling sales.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay's next stop is Philadelphia, where family run restaurant Chiarella's is struggling to survive in an area full of thriving Italian eateries. Tommy and Dina Defino's marriage is on the verge of collapse and his sense of personal failure only adds to the troubles of their crisis-ridden business.(n)

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube

programme.title: The Goldbergs

They recruit Adam to make a showreel about Bill, but Beverly insists she will find him someone to date in the old-fashioned way. Barry decides he wants to be a gym teacher and starts to act as Coach Mellor's apprentice. David Koechner and Allie Grant guest star.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam risks bodily harm during an intense game of paintball to impress his latest crush. Murray's stubborn behaviour causes Beverly to miss seeing the Beach Boys at Live Aid.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard's mother visits and he is horrified to learn that Sheldon knows more than he does about his own family, including his parents' impending divorce and the death of his dog. Guest starring Christine Baranski.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj lusts after a girl he and Sheldon meet at a university party, and Leonard comes to regret airing his disapproval of Penny's belief in psychics. Comedy, starring Kunal Nayyar and Jim Parsons.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Sheldon's flat is burgled, so they work on a state-of-the-art security system in a bid to outwit any future would-be thieves. However, their plans go awry when they ensnare Penny in a net. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki and Jim Parsons.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon develops a mental block and has difficulty working on a formula, so in a move inspired by Albert Einstein he gets a job at the Cheesecake Factory diner to try to revive his creativity - and despite Penny's objections, proves surprisingly effective in the role. Guest starring Yeardley Smith (The voice of Lisa Simpson).(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is invited to visit the Large Hadron Collider in Switzerland and asks Penny to join him as a Valentine's Day treat. However, Sheldon tries to persuade the couple to allow him to take her place.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon misses an opportunity to meet Spider-Man co-creator Stan Lee when he has to go to court for driving through a red light, and his anger leads to his imprisonment. Penny finds out the address of the comic-book writer and takes Sheldon there - but his troubles with the law have yet to reach their conclusion and the visit does not go as expected.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: An argument between Leonard and Sheldon over the ending of a Harry Potter book affects Amy and Penny's living arrangements. Meanwhile, Howard goes on a trip to Las Vegas with Bernadette and asks Raj to keep an eye on his mother while he is away.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Penny celebrate Valentine's Day, but their evening takes a turn for the worse when the latter's ex-boyfriend proposes to his girlfriend in the same restaurant. Meanwhile, Sheldon enlists the help of his assistant Alex to find the perfect gift for Amy.(n)

programme.title: Puss in Boots (2011)
programme.desc: The swashbuckling feline joins forces with a cunning thief and a treacherous old friend to steal a magical golden goose from a giant's castle. He must save the day when it transpires that their actions have unleashed a terrible danger on his home town. Animated comedy spin-off from the Shrek movies, with the voices of Antonio Banderas and Salma Hayek.(n)

programme.title: Big Brother: Best Shows Ever
programme.sub-title: The 'Would You Like Me to Be the Cat?' One
programme.desc: This episode re-joins the action in the CBB4 house to hear George Galloway utter those eternal words to Rula Lenska, ‘Would you like me to be the cat?'.(n)

programme.title: Batwoman
programme.desc: Kate and Luke are on the trail of a serial slasher targeting social media users, while Sophie gets an unexpected visit from her mother. Jacob is approached to make good on a favour, and Alice focuses on her plans for retribution.(n)

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson hosts a fresh run of the dating show. Student Charles is a self-confessed 'idiot' who says his love life is a disaster, so he hopes to find a partner by going naked. Lee, also a student, identifies as non-binary and pansexual and is open to dating all genders. She hopes to find someone who will accept her for who she is.(n)

programme.title: Supernatural
programme.desc: Sam and Dean's latest case forces them to look to occult lore - but brings much more than either of them anticipated when it leads to a reunion with their father. Jeffrey Dean Morgan returns as John Winchester for the 300th episode.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The friends fight over a ring they believe was used in the Lord of the Rings film trilogy. Leonard hopes to return the item to its rightful owner - but Raj wishes to sell it so he can buy a jet ski, and Sheldon develops a Gollum-like obsession with the precious object.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is awarded a prestigious prize by the university, but is scared of collecting the gong at a ceremony because he knows he'll have to give a speech. Penny tries to gee him up with rousing references to X-Men, and Leonard and Raj offer psychological guidance - without much success.(n)

programme.title: Big Brother: Best Shows Ever
programme.sub-title: The 'Would You Like Me to Be the Cat?' One

However, a bigger problem emerges when former classmates John Mabey and Martin Young renew old hostilities, so Scott tries to lighten the mood by taking everyone for a dance in 'Essex's smallest disco' - his kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Deb Croghan hosts the third dinner party in Chelmsford, Essex, and hopes to impress her guests with a tarts and vicars-style evening. Following an uncomfortable game of guessing everyone's age, she perks up the evening with an unusual entertainment - a cucumber-based sport she claims is a traditional family pastime.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Four in a Bed

Workaholic Alfie worries that having no phone signal will affect his business, while competitive Megan and Dick relish finding dust in the outside building.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competitors have great expectations of Kevin Wainwright and partner David Little's Satis House in Suffolk, but the last visit of the week sees old resentments resurface as Megan and Dick eagerly point out faults in the hosts' presentation - including an oversight in their room and overly crispy bacon for breakfast.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
